sections.sectionTitle: ELECTRONIC CONSTRUCTION AND FACILITY SUPPORT CONTRACT MANAGEMENT SYSTEM
sections.aiSummary: Specification section 01 31 23.13 20 — ELECTRONIC CONSTRUCTION AND FACILITY SUPPORT CONTRACT MANAGEMENT SYSTEM — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

Specification section 01 32 17.00 20 — COST-LOADED NETWORK ANALYSIS SCHEDULES (NAS) — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

Section 01 33 00 establishes the submittal procedures and quality control documentation requirements for the TIER 3 hybrid dry dock construction at Naval Station Neverland. This section is critical for QC as it defines what must be submitted, approved, and verified before work proceeds, ensuring compliance with naval construction standards and government oversight at key decision points.

requirements.title: Preconstruction Conference & Project Schedule
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed project schedule, site logistics plan, and QC/QA organization chart for Government approval prior to mobilization. Schedule must identify all critical path activities, long-lead procurement items, and dry dock operational windows.

requirements.title: Shop Drawings – Structural Steel & Fabrication
requirements.description: All structural steel shop drawings, connection details, and welding procedures shall be submitted for Government review and approval. Includes dry dock gate structural members, pump house frame, and support pile reinforcement details.

requirements.title: Concrete Compressive Strength – Dry Dock Foundation
requirements.description: Cylinder tests shall be performed on concrete poured for dry dock foundation and structural base. Testing required at 7-day and 28-day curing intervals.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per 100 cubic yards, minimum 2 per pour
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Minimum 3500 psi at 7 days; minimum 4500 psi at 28 days

requirements.title: Product Data – Mechanical & Electrical Equipment
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit manufacturer product data, certifications, and performance specifications for all dry dock pumps, motors, control systems, and electrical distribution equipment prior to procurement.

requirements.title: Welder Qualification Certificates & NDT Inspection Reports
requirements.description: All welders performing structural and pressure boundary welds shall provide current AWS D1.1 certification. Ultrasonic and radiographic inspection reports for critical welds shall be submitted and approved before final acceptance.

requirements.title: Hydrostatic Test – Dry Dock Caisson & Gate Systems
requirements.description: Full hydrostatic test of dry dock caisson, gate seals, and associated piping systems shall be performed and documented before operational handover.
requirements.testStandard: ASME Section VIII; Navy NAVSEA Technical Manual
requirements.testFrequency: 1 test per system completion
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Zero visible leakage at design head pressure; gate operation within specification tolerances

requirements.title: Coating & Corrosion Protection Inspection Reports
requirements.description: Surface preparation, primer application, and finish coat inspection reports for all structural steel and underwater components. Dry film thickness (DFT) measurements and holiday testing shall be documented and approved.

requirements.title: Operation & Maintenance Manuals – As-Built
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit final O&M documentation for dry dock pumping systems, gate control systems, and auxiliary equipment. Manuals shall include as-built drawings, spare parts lists, and maintenance schedules.

sections.aiSummary: This section establishes the mandatory submittal procedures, schedules, and approval protocols for all construction materials, shop drawings, test reports, and certifications on the TIER 3 hybrid dry dock project. Rigorous submittal QC is critical to prevent schedule delays, ensure regulatory compliance with NAVSEA standards, and maintain traceability of all Government-furnished and Contractor-supplied equipment and systems.

requirements.title: Preconstruction Submittals and Project Schedules
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed construction schedule, quality control plan, submittals schedule, and site safety plan within 14 days of NTP. All submittals must identify critical path activities and dry dock operational dependencies.

requirements.title: Shop Drawings for Structural Steel and Marine Systems
requirements.description: All shop drawings for dry dock structural components, caisson systems, and naval-grade mechanical/electrical systems require Government approval prior to fabrication. Drawings must include stress calculations, weld procedures per NAVSEA standards, and material certs.

requirements.title: Product Data and Manufacturer Technical Documentation
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit manufacturer technical data sheets, performance specifications, and operational manuals for all COTS (Commercial Off-The-Shelf) equipment including pumping systems, gate mechanisms, and control systems at least 21 days before delivery to site.

requirements.title: Material Sample Approval (Coatings and Concrete)
requirements.description: Representative samples of all marine epoxy coatings, concrete mixes for submerged elements, and stainless steel fasteners must be approved by Government prior to bulk material procurement. Samples shall include color standards and durability test data.

requirements.title: Concrete Compressive Strength Testing (Dry Dock Basin)
requirements.description: All concrete for dry dock basin, pump foundation pads, and structural elements shall be tested for compressive strength per ASTM C39.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 sample per 50 cubic yards or once per day, whichever is more frequent
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Minimum 4,000 psi at 28 days; minimum 90% of design strength at 7 days

requirements.title: Steel Weld Inspection and NDT Test Reports
requirements.description: Certified NDT reports (radiography and ultrasonic) for all critical structural welds on caisson, gates, and pressure vessels must be submitted within 5 days of welding completion. Reports must comply with NAVSEA Technical Manual and AWS D1.1.

requirements.title: Material Certificates of Conformance and Mill Certs
requirements.description: Certified material test reports (MTRs) and certificates of conformance from mills and suppliers for structural steel, fasteners, and specialty materials must accompany all deliveries and be logged in the QC database before acceptance.

requirements.title: Closeout Documentation and As-Built Records
requirements.description: Prior to final acceptance, Contractor shall submit complete as-built drawings, operation and maintenance manuals, spare parts lists, warranty documentation, and photographic record of all embedded/concealed work. All submittals must be organized per the Submittals Schedule.

Specification section 01 33 15 — ALTERNATIVE TECHNICAL CONCEPT DESIGN SUBMITTALS — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

Specification section 01 35 13 — SPECIAL PROJECT PROCEDURES — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

This section establishes mandatory governmental safety requirements, protocols, and compliance documentation for TIER 3 hybrid dry dock construction at Naval Station Neverland, including OSHA coordination, facility-specific safety plans, and Government witness/hold points for critical safety-related construction activities. QC verification ensures all safety submittals, certifications, and inspections meet Federal and Navy standards before work proceeds.

requirements.title: Site-Specific Safety and Health Plan
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit comprehensive site-specific safety plan addressing dry dock hazards, confined spaces, fall protection, rigging operations, and Navy-specific security protocols. Plan must address OSHA 1915 (shipyard) and 1926 (construction) standards applicable to hybrid dry dock work.

requirements.title: Respiratory Protection Program Documentation
requirements.description: Submit evidence of OSHA-compliant respiratory protection program including fit-testing records, cartridge compatibility matrices, and industrial hygiene baseline survey for dry dock air quality assessment.

requirements.title: Competent Rigger and Signalperson Certifications
requirements.description: Provide current certifications (within 3 years) for all personnel performing rigging, hoisting, and signaling operations on the hybrid dry dock project. Certifications must comply with ASME B30.20 or equivalent Navy-approved standards.

requirements.title: Fall Protection System Inspection
requirements.description: Government QC witness required for initial installation and monthly inspections of all fall arrest systems, guardrails, and safety net installations in accordance with OSHA 1926.500 and Navy Technical Manual requirements.

requirements.title: Confined Space Entry Authorization
requirements.description: No personnel shall enter confined spaces (tanks, voids, machinery spaces) until Government approval of atmospheric testing results and entry permit procedures per OSHA 1910.146 and Navy confined space protocols.

requirements.title: Atmospheric Monitoring – Confined Space Air Quality
requirements.description: Pre-entry atmospheric testing of all designated confined spaces to verify oxygen levels, explosive gases, and toxic vapor concentrations.
requirements.testFrequency: Before each entry event; daily if space remains open
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Oxygen 19.5–23.5%; LEL <10%; H₂S <10 ppm; CO <35 ppm

requirements.title: Weekly Safety Inspection Report
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit weekly documented safety inspections covering housekeeping, PPE compliance, equipment condition, and near-miss incidents. Government retains right to audit and issue corrective action notices.

requirements.title: Scaffolding Competent Person Certification and Design Approval
requirements.description: Submit current OSHA 1926.454 competent person card and engineered scaffolding design drawings (sealed by PE) specific to dry dock geometry and load conditions. Government must approve before erection.

sections.aiSummary: Specification section 01 42 00 — SOURCES FOR REFERENCE PUBLICATIONS — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

sections.sectionTitle: QUALITY CONTROL FOR PART A (DBB) WORK
sections.aiSummary: This section establishes quality control procedures, inspection protocols, and acceptance criteria for all Design-Bid-Build (DBB) work performed during TIER 3 hybrid dry dock construction at Naval Station Neverland. QC oversight ensures compliance with contract documents, regulatory standards, and Navy specifications before, during, and after construction activities.

requirements.title: Quality Control Plan
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit a comprehensive QC Plan detailing organizational structure, inspection procedures, testing protocols, hold points, witness points, and corrective action procedures for all DBB work phases.

requirements.title: Preconstruction Conference Documentation
requirements.description: Contractor shall document preconstruction meeting attendance, QC personnel qualifications, inspection schedules, and Government witness point coordination procedures.

requirements.title: Concrete Compressive Strength Testing
requirements.description: QC shall perform compressive strength tests on concrete samples from all structural and non-structural concrete placements in dry dock and support structures.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per 100 cubic yards or 1 per day, whichever is more frequent
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Minimum 4,000 psi at 28 days; no single test result below 90% of specified strength

requirements.title: Structural Steel Welding Inspection
requirements.description: Government representative shall witness all structural steel welding operations, including visual inspection, welder qualifications verification, and non-destructive testing preparation.

requirements.title: Reinforcing Steel Placement Verification
requirements.description: QC shall inspect and photograph all reinforcing steel placement, spacing, cover, and tie installation before concrete placement; Government approval required before pour.

requirements.title: Steel Paint System Dry Film Thickness
requirements.description: QC shall measure dry film thickness (DFT) of all structural steel coating systems using electromagnetic gauge to verify compliance with paint specification.
requirements.testFrequency: Minimum 3 readings per 1,000 square feet per coat
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: DFT within ±10% of specification; minimum coverage per primer, intermediate, and topcoat requirements

requirements.title: Daily QC Inspection Report
requirements.description: Contractor QC Manager shall submit daily inspection reports documenting activities inspected, deficiencies identified, corrective actions issued, and hold/witness point status.

requirements.title: Third-Party Testing Laboratory Certifications
requirements.description: All concrete and steel testing shall be performed by Government-approved third-party laboratories; certifications and accreditations must be current and submitted prior to testing.

sections.sectionTitle: CAISSON AND CAISSON SEAT GEOMETRY QUALITY CONTROL
sections.aiSummary: This section establishes QC procedures for verification of caisson geometry, seat preparation, and alignment tolerances critical to dry dock structural integrity and vessel support during maintenance operations. Precise caisson positioning and seat flatness directly impact load distribution, vessel stability, and safety compliance for TIER 3 hybrid dry dock operations.

requirements.title: Caisson and Seat Geometry Control Plan
requirements.description: Contractor submits detailed QC plan including measurement protocols, survey baseline establishment, tolerance matrices for caisson seat elevation/flatness, and corrective action thresholds. Plan must reference Navy Dry Dock Technical Manual and project structural design requirements.

requirements.title: Caisson Seat Surface Preparation Inspection
requirements.description: QC Manager verifies caisson seat concrete surface cleanliness, removal of laitance, and any spalling or voids prior to caisson placement. Visual inspection and surface profile measurement required.

requirements.title: Caisson Seat Flatness Survey
requirements.description: Licensed surveyor performs precision elevation survey of caisson seat surface using total station or laser theodolite per ASTM E1489 methodology. Grid spacing minimum 10 feet in both directions.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per caisson seat (minimum 3 datum points per 100 SF)
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Maximum 0.25 inches deviation from design elevation over 10-foot span; no point exceeds ±0.50 inches from design datum

requirements.title: Caisson Alignment and Plumb Verification
requirements.description: Measurement of caisson vertical plumb, athwartship and fore-aft centerline alignment relative to dry dock structural grid using laser theodolite or transit.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per caisson placement; minimum 4 corner measurements
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Plumb tolerance ±0.125 inches per 50 feet height; centerline alignment ±0.25 inches

requirements.title: Caisson Seat Concrete Strength Verification
requirements.description: Government witnesses extraction of concrete core samples from caisson seat minimum 48 hours prior to caisson placement. Cores tested for minimum compressive strength.
requirements.testFrequency: Minimum 1 core per 500 SF of seat, minimum 3 cores total per caisson
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Minimum 4500 psi compressive strength at time of testing

requirements.title: Caisson Geometry Compliance Documentation
requirements.description: QC Manager compiles as-built survey data, test results, photographic evidence, and deviation logs. Report certifies compliance with design geometry or documents approved deviations with Engineer-approved corrective actions.

requirements.title: Caisson Installation Readiness Certificate
requirements.description: Professional surveyor and Contractor QC Manager jointly certify that caisson seat preparation, geometry, and alignment meet or exceed contract specifications. Government signature required before caisson float-in or placement operations commence.

sections.sectionTitle: DESIGN AND CONSTRUCTION QUALITY CONTROL (FOR PART B – DB WORK)
sections.aiSummary: This section establishes the Design-Builder's quality control plan, testing protocols, and documentation requirements for all construction work on the TIER 3 hybrid dry dock project. It ensures compliance with naval construction standards, material certifications, and structural performance requirements through systematic inspections, testing, and Government witness/hold points.

requirements.title: Quality Control Plan (DB Work)
requirements.description: Design-Builder shall submit a comprehensive QC plan identifying organizational structure, inspection schedules, testing protocols, and corrective action procedures for all DB-performed construction activities. Plan must address concrete, steel, mechanical systems, and dry dock structural elements.

requirements.title: Concrete Mix Design and Material Certifications
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit concrete mix designs, admixture certifications, and supplier mill test reports for all concrete designated for structural and marine-exposure elements. All materials must meet ASTM C150 Type II or Type V as specified for dry dock environment.

requirements.title: Concrete Compressive Strength Testing
requirements.description: Concrete cylinders cast and tested per ASTM C31 and C39 for all structural concrete placements in the dry dock basin and supporting structures.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 set per 100 CY or once per day, whichever is more frequent
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Minimum 4,000 psi at 28 days for marine-exposure elements; minimum 3,500 psi for non-exposure structural concrete

requirements.title: Structural Steel Fit-Up and Connection Inspection
requirements.description: Government QC representative shall witness all primary structural steel connections, including bolted and welded connections on the dry dock frame and gate support systems. Tolerance verification and NDT scheduling shall be coordinated with Government.

requirements.title: Weld Procedure and Welder Qualification
requirements.description: Contractor shall perform bend tests and radiographic inspection on production welds per ASME Section VIII and AWS D1.1 standards for all pressure boundary and structural welds.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 coupon per welder per position; radiography on 10% of primary structural welds minimum
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: No cracks, porosity >3/8 in. in length, or undercut >0.03 in. depth; visual acceptance per AWS D1.1 Section 5

requirements.title: Material Certifications and Test Reports
requirements.description: Contractor shall furnish certified mill test reports (MTRs) and third-party material certifications for all structural steel, fasteners, and specialty alloys before incorporation into the work. All certificates shall trace to actual material heats and lots.

requirements.title: Weekly QC Progress and Deficiency Reports
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit weekly QC reports documenting completed inspections, test results, nonconformances identified, and corrective actions. Reports shall identify any conditions affecting schedule or structural integrity.

requirements.title: Dry Dock Basin Waterproofing and Coating System Inspection
requirements.description: All coating systems and waterproofing membranes in the dry dock basin must pass Government surface preparation, holiday detection, and adhesion testing before final acceptance. Surface profile, paint thickness, and continuity shall be verified.

sections.aiSummary: Section 01 45 35 establishes mandatory special inspection and testing protocols for critical structural, concrete, and welding work on the TIER 3 hybrid dry dock. These inspections ensure compliance with ACI, AWS, and AISC standards and provide Government QC verification at key construction milestones to maintain structural integrity and safety compliance.

requirements.title: Special Inspections and Testing Plan
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit a comprehensive Special Inspections Plan detailing all required inspections, testing agencies, inspection schedules, hold points, witness points, and acceptance criteria for structural concrete, structural steel welding, and mechanical systems.

requirements.title: Certified Independent Testing Agency Letter of Appointment
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit evidence of engagement with a Government-approved, AISC-certified independent testing laboratory authorized to perform structural inspection and materials testing on naval construction projects.

requirements.title: Concrete Compressive Strength Testing
requirements.description: Compressive strength tests shall be performed on concrete cylinders from all structural concrete pours, including dry dock basin floor, walls, and foundation elements.
requirements.testFrequency: 2 samples per 100 cubic yards, minimum 1 per pour
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Minimum 4000 psi at 28 days; no single sample below 90% of f'c; average of 2 cylinders ≥ f'c

requirements.title: Structural Steel Welding Visual and UT Inspection
requirements.description: Government QC and independent testing agency shall witness all structural steel welding of primary load-bearing members, gate frames, and critical dry dock infrastructure. 100% visual inspection and minimum 10% ultrasonic testing per AWS D1.1 Section 5.
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: No cracks, incomplete fusion, or porosity exceeding AWS D1.1 Table 4.23 acceptance limits

requirements.title: Concrete Curing and Strength Verification Before Formwork Removal
requirements.description: Formwork and shoring shall not be removed from structural concrete elements until 28-day compressive strength is verified and minimum 75% of design strength is achieved (per ACI 301).
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: ≥75% of f'c minimum before removal; notification to Government required before proceeding

requirements.title: Reinforcing Steel Mill Certifications and Bend Tests
requirements.description: All reinforcing steel materials shall be accompanied by mill test reports and samples shall be subjected to bend testing per ASTM A615 to verify mechanical properties and weldability.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 bend test per 50 tons per grade and size
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: No cracks visible after bending through 180° around mandrel per ASTM A370

requirements.title: Soils and Foundation Investigation Report
requirements.description: Licensed geotechnical engineer shall submit final soil testing and foundation inspection report, including bearing capacity verification, settlement analysis, and subsurface conditions confirmation for dry dock foundations.

requirements.title: Independent Testing Agency Final Inspection Certificate
requirements.description: Upon completion of all special inspections and testing, the independent testing agency shall issue a Final Certificate of Compliance affirming that all structural elements, welds, concrete, and systems meet or exceed design specifications and applicable codes.

sections.aiSummary: Specification section 01 50 00 — TEMPORARY CONSTRUCTION FACILITIES AND CONTROLS — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

Section 01 57 19 governs temporary environmental controls including dust suppression, air quality monitoring, noise abatement, and stormwater pollution prevention during hybrid dry dock construction. Effective environmental controls are critical for NAVFAC compliance, worker safety, and protection of the Naval Station Neverland marine environment.

requirements.title: Stormwater Pollution Prevention Plan (SWPPP)
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit comprehensive SWPPP detailing erosion control, sediment barriers, storm drain inlet protection, concrete washout areas, and fueling/material storage containment specific to dry dock perimeter and marine interface zones.

requirements.title: Dust Control and Air Quality System Specifications
requirements.description: Product data for dust suppression equipment (mist systems, tarping, vacuum collection) and air filtration units with MERV ratings and noise attenuation specifications for operation near active naval base areas.

requirements.title: Air Monitoring Equipment Calibration Certificate
requirements.description: Third-party calibration certificate for particulate matter (PM10/PM2.5) monitors, sound level meters, and volatile organic compound (VOC) detectors used for continuous environmental monitoring throughout construction duration.

requirements.title: Temporary Environmental Control System Activation
requirements.description: Government QC representative shall witness initial startup and verification of all dust suppression, stormwater containment, noise barriers, and air monitoring systems before any demolition or structural work begins in the dry dock.

requirements.title: Weekly Ambient Air Quality Baseline Survey
requirements.description: Contractor shall conduct weekly particulate and VOC air quality sampling at three perimeter locations and one off-site control location.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per week throughout construction
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: PM10 ≤ 150 µg/m³ daily average; VOC levels within OSHA 8-hour TWA limits

requirements.title: Monthly Environmental Compliance Report
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit monthly documentation of dust suppression maintenance logs, stormwater inspection records, noise monitoring data, spill incident reports, and corrective actions taken for any non-compliance.

requirements.title: Secondary Containment Inspection and Approval
requirements.description: All temporary fuel tanks, hydraulic fluid storage, paint lockers, and hazardous material staging areas shall be inspected and approved by Government QC before materials are introduced; 100% containment volume verification required.

requirements.title: Environmental Control Operation and Maintenance Procedures
requirements.description: Contractor-prepared procedures for daily operation, weekly inspection, maintenance schedules, and emergency shut-down protocols for all dust, noise, and stormwater control systems; includes responsible personnel assignments.

sections.aiSummary: Specification section 01 57 19.01 20 — SUPPLEMENTAL TEMPORARY ENVIRONMENTAL CONTROLS — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.
Specification section 01 58 00 — PROJECT IDENTIFICATION — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

Section 01 74 19 establishes mandatory waste segregation, tracking, and disposal protocols for construction debris generated during TIER 3 hybrid dry dock construction at Naval Station Neverland. This specification ensures environmental compliance, hazardous material control, and accurate waste stream documentation required by Navy and federal regulations, critical for QC sign-off on daily operations and final closeout.

requirements.title: Construction Waste Management Plan
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit a detailed Waste Management Plan identifying waste streams (metals, concrete, wood, hazardous), segregation methods, on-site staging areas, disposal vendors, and tracking procedures. Plan must address dry dock-specific challenges including marine debris and abrasive blast waste.

requirements.title: Waste Hauler and Disposal Facility Certifications
requirements.description: Prior to any waste removal, Contractor shall provide current certifications and licenses for all waste hauling companies and disposal facilities, including environmental permits and waste stream authorization documentation.

requirements.title: Weekly Waste Inventory and Disposal Logs
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit weekly reports documenting weight/volume of each waste stream segregated, dates staged, vendor removed waste, and destination facility. Reports must be submitted every Friday for the preceding week's activities.

requirements.title: Daily Waste Staging Area Inspection
requirements.description: QC Manager shall inspect designated waste staging areas daily to verify proper segregation, containment integrity, labeling compliance, and prevention of environmental release. Any commingled or improperly staged waste halts further disposal until corrected.

requirements.title: Hazardous Waste Characterization and Manifesting
requirements.description: Government witness required at point of identification and packaging of any suspect hazardous waste (blast residue, coatings, solvents, batteries). Contractor shall provide pre-staged material samples for Government testing if contamination is suspected.

requirements.title: Abrasive Blast Waste Characterization
requirements.description: Representative samples of blast residue shall be collected and tested for heavy metal content and TCLP compliance prior to disposal route finalization.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per 100 tons of blast waste generated
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Non-hazardous classification per EPA; no constituent exceeds TCLP threshold limits

requirements.title: Final Waste Disposition Certifications
requirements.description: At project closeout, Contractor shall provide final manifests, disposal receipts, and certifications from all waste facilities confirming receipt and proper disposal of all segregated waste streams. Certificate of Waste Diversion percentage and landfill diversion statistics required.

sections.aiSummary: Section 01 78 00 governs all closeout submittals required before final acceptance of the TIER 3 hybrid dry dock at Naval Station Neverland, including as-built documentation, O&M manuals, warranties, and final inspection certifications. Proper closeout submittal management ensures complete contractual compliance, operational readiness, and establishes the baseline for warranty and maintenance obligations.

requirements.title: As-Built Drawings and Record Documents
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit complete as-built drawings reflecting all changes, modifications, and actual field conditions for the dry dock structure, mechanical systems, electrical systems, and HVAC. All mark-ups must be legible, dated, and signed by the on-site supervisor.

requirements.title: Operation and Maintenance Manuals
requirements.description: Complete O&M documentation for all equipment including dry dock pumping systems, electrical distribution, HVAC, fire suppression, and safety systems, organized by equipment type with index and quick-reference guides.

requirements.title: Warranties and Guarantees Compilation
requirements.description: Contractor certification and compilation of all manufacturer warranties, extended warranties, and performance bonds for equipment and systems, indexed by location and equipment type with copy of proof of purchase or transfer.

requirements.title: Final Inspection and Acceptance Certificate
requirements.description: Government QC Manager sign-off certificate confirming all work meets specifications, all deficiencies corrected, and facility is operationally ready for Navy acceptance.

requirements.title: Punch List and Deficiency Resolution Report
requirements.description: Comprehensive list of all identified deficiencies during final inspection with corrective actions taken, photographs of corrections, and sign-off dates. Report must be organized by trade and severity level.

requirements.title: Spare Parts and Maintenance Kit Documentation
requirements.description: Inventory listing of all spare parts, consumables, and maintenance kits provided by contractor with locations, part numbers, quantities, and shelf-life information for first-year operations.

requirements.title: LEED/Sustainability Performance Verification
requirements.description: Certification documentation for all TIER 3 sustainable design features including water management systems, energy efficiency measures, and environmental compliance for the hybrid dry dock facility.

sections.aiSummary: Specification section 01 78 23 — OPERATION AND MAINTENANCE DATA — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

sections.sectionTitle: FACILITY ELECTRONIC OPERATION AND MAINTENANCE SUPPORT INFORMATION (eOMSI)
sections.aiSummary: Specification section 01 78 24.00 20 — FACILITY ELECTRONIC OPERATION AND MAINTENANCE SUPPORT INFORMATION (eOMSI) — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.
Specification section 01 91 00.15 20 — TOTAL BUILDING COMMISSIONING — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

Section 02 41 00 governs the safe, compliant demolition and removal of existing structures, utilities, and hazardous materials at Naval Station Neverland hybrid dry dock site. QC oversight ensures environmental compliance, structural safety, proper waste stream segregation, and verification that the site is prepared to receive new construction without delay or rework.

requirements.title: Demolition Plan and Hazardous Materials Survey
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit a comprehensive demolition plan including sequencing, equipment staging, utility disconnection procedures, and results of Phase I/II environmental and hazmat survey. Plan must address asbestos, lead paint, PCB detection, and naval-specific contamination protocols.

requirements.title: Waste Segregation and Disposal Certifications
requirements.description: Contractor shall provide manifests, tipping tickets, and third-party certifications for all demolition waste streams (concrete, metal, hazardous, salvage) documenting proper disposition per EPA and NAVFAC environmental requirements.

requirements.title: Utility Disconnection Verification
requirements.description: All electrical, water, sewer, steam, chilled water, and fire protection systems must be isolated, capped, and verified by Contractor and Government QC prior to any structural demolition. Naval-specific systems (compressed air, weapon-handling hydraulics) require Commander Naval Facilities Engineering Command (NAVFAC) technical sign-off.

requirements.title: Asbestos/Hazmat Abatement Completion Inspection
requirements.description: Government QC Inspector shall witness final clearance air sampling and receipt of certified abatement completion reports before non-hazmat demolition operations commence. Clearance must demonstrate <0.01 f/cc airborne asbestos.

requirements.title: Structural Stability and Vibration Monitoring During Demolition
requirements.description: Contractor shall perform real-time vibration monitoring during blasting or mechanical demolition to ensure adjacent naval facilities (wet basin, power plants, barracks) remain within safe vibration thresholds.
requirements.testFrequency: Continuous during demolition events; baseline pre-demolition survey required
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Peak particle velocity ≤2.0 in/sec at property line; ≤1.0 in/sec at operational naval facilities

requirements.title: Daily Demolition Progress and Environmental Compliance Report
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit daily reports documenting quantities demolished, waste segregation completion, any spills/releases, air/water quality observations, and certifications of dust control, noise abatement, and traffic management compliance.

requirements.title: Final Demolition Closeout and Site Clearance Certificate
requirements.description: Contractor and third-party environmental firm shall jointly certify that all demolition is complete, debris removed, utilities properly terminated, soil/groundwater testing completed per scope, and site is clean for new construction. Must include photo documentation and as-built survey.

requirements.title: Contractor's Safety and Health Plan (Naval-Specific)
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit OSHA 10 CFR 1926 compliant SHSP addressing fall protection, confined space entry, naval security protocols, explosive ordnance disposal (EOD) liaison procedures, and emergency response for active dry dock operations in adjacent basins.

sections.sectionTitle: EXCAVATION AND HANDLING OF CONTAMINATED MATERIAL
sections.aiSummary: Section 02 61 13 governs the safe excavation, segregation, handling, and disposal of contaminated soil and groundwater encountered during dry dock construction at Naval Station Neverland. QC must verify contamination assessment protocols, worker safety compliance, and proper documentation of waste streams to ensure environmental regulatory compliance and protect personnel.

requirements.title: Contaminated Material Management Plan
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit a detailed plan identifying contamination screening procedures, excavation sequencing, on-site segregation areas, decontamination protocols, and disposal pathways for anticipated contaminated soils and groundwater.

requirements.title: Laboratory Qualifications and Analytical Methods
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit proof of NELAP-certified or EPA-accredited laboratory credentials and analytical methods to be used for on-site and confirmatory soil and groundwater sampling.

requirements.title: Baseline Soil Contamination Screening
requirements.description: Field screening of excavated material using calibrated photoionization detector (PID) or equivalent to identify volatile organic compounds (VOCs) and establish baseline contamination levels.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per 100 linear feet of excavation or 1 per soil horizon change
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Results logged and compared to site remedial action levels (RALs); exceedances trigger laboratory confirmation and segregation

requirements.title: Laboratory Confirmation Sampling
requirements.description: Collection and analysis of soil samples from field-screened contaminated areas to quantify heavy metals, petroleum hydrocarbons, and chlorinated solvents per analytical scope.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 composite per 250 CY of segregated material or per contamination hot spot
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Results shall not exceed state remedial action levels; waste classification determines final disposal pathway

requirements.title: Excavation and Segregation Operations
requirements.description: QC Inspector shall witness all excavation activities in known or suspected contaminated zones, verification of segregation into clean fill, hazardous waste, and non-hazardous waste stockpiles, and documentation of excavation depths and lateral extents.

requirements.title: Daily Contaminated Material Log
requirements.description: Contractor shall maintain and submit daily logs documenting volumes excavated, segregation actions taken, field screening results, sample locations, and disposal manifests for all contaminated material removed from the site.

requirements.title: Waste Disposal Manifests and Certifications
requirements.description: Contractor shall provide signed waste disposal certifications, manifests, and final destination confirmations from licensed landfills or treatment facilities for all contaminated soil and waste water streams.

requirements.title: Worker Health and Safety Plan (Contamination-Specific)
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit OSHA-compliant Health and Safety Plan addressing respiratory protection, decontamination stations, air monitoring protocols, and medical surveillance for personnel handling contaminated materials.

sections.sectionTitle: REMOVAL AND DISPOSAL OF PCB CONTAMINATED SOILS
sections.aiSummary: This section establishes QC procedures for identification, excavation, sampling, testing, and disposal of PCB-contaminated soils discovered during TIER 3 hybrid dry dock construction at Naval Station Neverland. Proper management of PCB soils is critical to meet EPA regulations, protect worker health, and maintain project schedule and budget.

requirements.title: PCB Soil Management Plan
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit a detailed PCB Soil Management Plan addressing soil survey methodology, excavation procedures, waste characterization protocols, disposal facility approval, and regulatory compliance documentation.

requirements.title: Contaminated Soil Excavation and Staging Plan
requirements.description: Plan shall identify excavation boundaries, staging areas, equipment decontamination procedures, personnel safety protocols, and daily reporting requirements for all PCB soil removal activities.

requirements.title: Baseline and Confirmation Soil Sampling
requirements.description: Soil samples collected from excavation areas and remaining soil profile shall be analyzed for total PCB concentration per EPA Method 8082B.
requirements.testFrequency: Minimum 1 sample per 500 cubic yards excavated; confirmation samples at 100% of boundaries
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: PCB concentration ≤ 1 ppm for unrestricted reuse; disposal if >1 ppm or per Navy/EPA directive

requirements.title: Disposal Facility Approval Certificate
requirements.description: Contractor shall provide EPA-licensed waste disposal facility authorization letter, manifest forms, and proof of facility's PCB waste acceptance prior to transport.

requirements.title: Excavation and Load-Out Operations
requirements.description: Government QC representative shall witness and document excavation boundaries, soil segregation, packaging, labeling, and vehicular load-out of PCB-contaminated soils.

requirements.title: PCB Soil Removal Daily Log and Final Disposition Report
requirements.description: Contractor shall maintain daily excavation logs including volume removed, sample results, weather conditions, and personnel. Final report shall certify total tonnage removed and disposal facility documentation.

requirements.title: Disposal Manifest and Waste Tracking Certification
requirements.description: Contractor shall provide signed EPA disposal manifests, weight tickets, and transporter certifications confirming PCB soil delivery to approved facility.

requirements.title: Soil Remediation Clearance Approval
requirements.description: Contaminated area shall not be backfilled or construction resumed until Government and environmental compliance officer approves final confirmation sampling results and clearance documentation.

sections.sectionTitle: TRANSPORTATION AND DISPOSAL OF HAZARDOUS MATERIALS
sections.aiSummary: Section 02 81 00 governs the safe identification, handling, transportation, and disposal of hazardous materials encountered or generated during hybrid dry dock construction at Naval Station Neverland, including contaminated soils, asbestos-containing materials, lead-based paint, and petroleum products. This section is critical for QC because improper management creates environmental liability, regulatory violations, worker safety risks, and project delays that directly impact Navy operations and Federal compliance.

requirements.title: Hazardous Materials Management Plan
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit a comprehensive plan identifying all known and potential hazardous materials on site, worker safety protocols, storage requirements, spill response procedures, and disposal methods in accordance with EPA, DOT, and Navy environmental regulations.

requirements.title: Pre-Construction Phase I Environmental Site Assessment
requirements.description: Qualified environmental consultant shall perform and submit Phase I ESA identifying baseline hazardous material conditions, historical operations data, and regulatory compliance status before any ground disturbance or demolition begins.

requirements.title: Asbestos Survey and Abatement Certification
requirements.description: Licensed asbestos abatement contractor shall provide certification of ACM inspection, abatement work, air clearance testing, and proper disposal documentation for any asbestos-containing materials removed during dry dock renovation.

requirements.title: Lead-Based Paint Removal and Containment
requirements.description: QC Manager shall witness all lead-based paint removal operations, including containment setup verification, worker respiratory protection compliance, and waste segregation before transport to licensed disposal facility.

requirements.title: Soil Contamination Testing for Petroleum Hydrocarbons
requirements.description: Independent laboratory shall test excavated soil samples for petroleum contamination levels and regulatory threshold exceedances to determine if material requires special handling or off-site disposal.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 composite sample per 500 cubic yards of excavation
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Results shall not exceed Navy Region Environmental regulations; remedial action required if TPH exceeds 100 mg/kg

requirements.title: Hazardous Waste Transportation and Disposal Records
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit manifests, bills of lading, and disposal facility receipts for all hazardous materials transported off-site, including chain-of-custody documentation and final disposal certifications.

requirements.title: Final Environmental Clearance and Site Closure
requirements.description: Government environmental officer shall inspect final site conditions, verify all hazardous materials have been properly removed or remediated, and issue clearance certificate before dry dock project closeout and Navy reinstatement.

sections.aiSummary: Section 02 82 00 governs the safe identification, encapsulation, and removal of asbestos-containing materials (ACM) prior to dry dock modifications at Naval Station Neverland. This section ensures compliance with EPA, OSHA, and Navy environmental standards to protect worker safety and prevent airborne asbestos contamination during construction.

requirements.title: Asbestos Survey and Assessment Report
requirements.description: Certified asbestos inspector must complete pre-remediation survey identifying all ACM locations, quantities, and condition within the dry dock structure. Report must include photographs, material sample analysis, and remediation prioritization.

requirements.title: Asbestos Remediation Work Plan
requirements.description: Contractor-prepared plan detailing abatement methodology, containment procedures, personal protective equipment (PPE) requirements, air monitoring protocols, and waste disposal procedures per EPA NESHAP standards.

requirements.title: Licensed Asbestos Contractor Certification
requirements.description: Verified credentials confirming contractor holds active EPA/state asbestos abatement license and worker certifications. Documentation must include insurance, training records for all personnel, and evidence of approved disposal facility contracts.

requirements.title: Pre-Remediation Air Quality Baseline
requirements.description: QC and Government representative must witness contractor's establishment of ambient asbestos fiber baseline measurements in work area and surrounding zones using OSHA Method AHERA sampling.

requirements.title: Encapsulation Effectiveness Testing
requirements.description: Following encapsulation of friable ACM, air sampling performed to verify airborne asbestos fiber concentrations remain below action levels throughout containment zone.
requirements.testFrequency: Daily during abatement; final clearance air samples
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Non-detectable or <0.01 f/cc (fibers per cubic centimeter) in work area

requirements.title: Daily Asbestos Work Activity Log
requirements.description: Contractor-certified daily logs documenting quantities of ACM removed, containment integrity checks, worker exposure monitoring results, waste tracking, and any incidents or deviations from the remediation plan.

requirements.title: Post-Abatement Clearance Certificate
requirements.description: Final certification from licensed asbestos professional confirming all identified ACM has been removed or properly encapsulated, post-remediation air sampling shows clearance, and work area is safe for construction resumption.

requirements.title: Asbestos Waste Disposal Documentation
requirements.description: Manifests and certificates from approved asbestos disposal facility confirming proper receipt, handling, and final disposition of all remediated ACM waste in compliance with DOT and EPA regulations.

sections.aiSummary: Section 02 83 00 governs the identification, containment, removal, and disposal of lead-based paint (LBP) and lead-contaminated materials during demolition and surface preparation at Naval Station Neverland's Tier 3 hybrid dry dock. Proper lead remediation is critical to protect worker health, comply with EPA RRP Rule and OSHA standards, and prevent environmental contamination of the marine facility.

requirements.title: Lead Remediation Plan and Health & Safety Protocol
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit a detailed Lead Remediation Plan identifying all lead-based materials, containment methods, worker protection measures, and waste disposal procedures. Plan must include site-specific HASP addressing lead exposure controls, personal protective equipment (PPE), air monitoring protocols, and waste characterization.

requirements.title: Lead Inspector and Risk Assessor Certification
requirements.description: Contractor shall provide current EPA-accredited Lead Inspector and Lead Risk Assessor certifications for personnel conducting lead inspections, sampling, and clearance testing. Certifications must be valid throughout the project duration.

requirements.title: Pre-Remediation Lead Paint XRF Analysis
requirements.description: Non-destructive XRF (X-Ray Fluorescence) testing to identify and map all lead-based paint on structural steel, bulkheads, and equipment surfaces within the dry dock work envelope.
requirements.testFrequency: Minimum 1 per 500 SF of painted surface; minimum 5 per structural element
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Lead concentration ≥1.0 mg/cm² or ≥0.5% by weight triggers LBP remediation protocol

requirements.title: Containment, Dust Control, and Negative Pressure Enclosure Design
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit engineering plans for temporary containment structures, plastic sheeting systems, negative air units (with HEPA filtration), decontamination stations, and waste accumulation areas. Plans must comply with EPA RRP Rule and OSHA 1926.62 lead exposure standards.

requirements.title: Air Monitoring (Personal and Area Samples)
requirements.description: Real-time and time-weighted average (TWA) air monitoring for lead particulates during all abrasive blasting, grinding, and removal activities to verify worker exposure remains below OSHA PEL (50 µg/m³ TWA).
requirements.testFrequency: Continuous during LBP removal operations; minimum 1 personal sample per worker shift
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Lead air concentration ≤50 µg/m³ (8-hour TWA); ≤200 µg/m³ (STEL)

requirements.title: Post-Remediation Clearance and Cleaning Verification Report
requirements.description: Lead Inspector shall document completion of remediation, clean-up verification via wipe sampling, HEPA vacuuming completion, and final visual inspection. Report must include photographs, wipe test results, and certification that surfaces meet EPA clearance standards.
requirements.testFrequency: Upon completion of all lead removal within each containment zone
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Wipe test results ≤10 µg/100 cm² for floors; ≤100 µg/100 cm² for interior structural components

requirements.title: Lead Waste Characterization and Disposal Documentation
requirements.description: Contractor shall provide manifests, waste characterization reports, and disposal facility certifications for all lead-contaminated materials, including painted debris, blast media, HEPA filters, and PPE. All disposal must comply with RCRA and local environmental regulations.

requirements.title: Worker Training Documentation and Lead Safety Certification
requirements.description: Contractor shall provide proof that all workers exposed to lead (abrasive blasters, laborers, supervisors) have completed EPA-approved or OSHA lead safety training. Documentation must include training dates, instructor credentials, and worker acknowledgment signatures.

sections.sectionTitle: HANDLING OF LIGHTING BALLASTS AND LAMPS CONTAINING PCBs AND MERCURY
sections.aiSummary: Specification section 02 84 16 — HANDLING OF LIGHTING BALLASTS AND LAMPS CONTAINING PCBs AND MERCURY — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

sections.sectionTitle: REMOVAL AND DISPOSAL OF POLYCHLORINATED BIPHENYLS (PCBs)
sections.aiSummary: Specification section 02 84 33 — REMOVAL AND DISPOSAL OF POLYCHLORINATED BIPHENYLS (PCBs) — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

Section 03 23 00 governs the design, fabrication, installation, and testing of all stressed tendon reinforcing systems (post-tensioning and prestressing) for the hybrid dry dock's reinforced concrete structural elements. QC oversight ensures tendon integrity, proper jacking procedures, and load verification to prevent structural failure and maintain design performance.

requirements.title: Post-Tensioning System Shop Drawings
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed shop drawings showing tendon routing, anchorage details, jacking points, load cells, and sequencing for all stressed tendon reinforcing. Drawings must include calculations for friction losses and wobble coefficients.

requirements.title: Tendon Material Product Data and Certifications
requirements.description: Submit mill test reports and certificates of conformance for all strand, wire, and bar products including tensile strength, elongation, and surface condition verification per ASTM A416 or A722.

requirements.title: Post-Tensioning Equipment and Procedure Manual
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit manufacturer instructions for all jacking equipment, load cells, and anchorages, including calibration certificates and pre-construction equipment verification procedures.

requirements.title: Pre-Jacking Tendon Inspection and Load Cell Calibration
requirements.description: QC and Government witness inspection of all installed tendon ducts, anchorages, and temporary supports prior to any jacking operations. All load cells must be calibrated within 90 days and verified operational.

requirements.title: Tendon Jacking Load Verification Test
requirements.description: Test each tendon group to verify actual jacking force matches design load and that tendon elongation is within ±6% of calculated theoretical elongation.
requirements.testStandard: ASTM E4 (Load Cell Calibration) / PCI Post-Tensioning Manual
requirements.testFrequency: 100% of all tendon groups
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Jacking load within ±2% of design load; elongation deviation ±6% of theoretical

requirements.title: Post-Tensioning Jacking Operations
requirements.description: Government QC and project Inspector shall witness all jacking operations, load cell readings, and elongation measurements. Inspector shall document loads, sequence, and any deviations in daily QC logs.

requirements.title: Anchorage Creep and Seating Loss Measurement
requirements.description: After jacking, measure tendon elongation loss over 72 hours to quantify seating losses in anchorages. Losses shall not exceed 2 mm per anchorage device.
requirements.testFrequency: 100% of all anchorage groups
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Seating loss ≤ 2 mm per anchorage; cumulative loss documented and approved

requirements.title: Post-Tensioning Completion Report
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit final report documenting all jacking records, load cell calibrations, elongation measurements, observed defects, and corrective actions. Report must include as-built tendon layout and final load certification.

sections.aiSummary: Section 03 30 00 governs all cast-in-place concrete construction for the TIER 3 hybrid dry dock, including material sourcing, mix design, placement, curing, and strength verification. QC oversight of concrete work is critical to structural integrity and naval vessel operational requirements.

requirements.title: Concrete Mix Design and Proportions
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit concrete mix design for Government approval, including water-cement ratio, aggregate gradation, admixture dosages, and 28-day compressive strength target of minimum 5,000 psi for structural elements.

requirements.title: Compressive Strength Testing (Cylinder Breaks)
requirements.description: Cast and test concrete cylinders per ASTM C31 at 7 days and 28 days to verify design strength compliance.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 set per 50 cubic yards or per placement, minimum 2 cylinders per set
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Minimum 5,000 psi at 28 days; 7-day strength minimum 75% of 28-day design

requirements.title: Concrete Slump and Air Content Verification
requirements.description: QC shall witness and document slump (per ASTM C143) and air content (per ASTM C173) testing at point of placement. Slump shall not exceed 4 inches; air content 4–6% for exposed structural elements.

requirements.title: Concrete Curing and Protection Plan
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed curing procedures, including temperature monitoring schedules, moisture retention methods, and protection protocols for ambient conditions at Naval Station Neverland.

requirements.title: Reinforcement and Embedded Item Inspection
requirements.description: QC shall inspect and approve all rebar placement, spacing, cover, ties, and embedded items (anchors, inserts) prior to concrete placement. Verify conformance to contract documents and ASTM A615.

requirements.title: Concrete Surface Hardness Testing (Rebound Hammer)
requirements.description: Non-destructive testing to assess concrete surface strength uniformity and cure adequacy after 7 days minimum.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 test per 500 square feet of exposed surface
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Rebound number ≥35 for naval-duty concrete

requirements.title: Concrete Construction Daily Logs and Placement Records
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit daily documentation of concrete placement, including volume, ambient temperature, time of pour, curing conditions, and any defects or corrective actions taken.

requirements.title: Concrete Supplier Mill Certificates and Material Certifications
requirements.description: Contractor certifies that all concrete materials (cement, aggregates, admixtures, water) comply with ASTM C150, C33, and project specifications, with supporting mill and test reports.

sections.aiSummary: Section 03 31 29 governs marine-grade concrete design, materials, and placement for the TIER 3 hybrid dry dock structure at Naval Station Neverland, with emphasis on durability testing in aggressive saltwater and chloride-laden environments. QC must track concrete strength, durability parameters (chloride penetration, freeze-thaw resistance), and environmental exposure compliance throughout production and construction phases.

requirements.title: Concrete Mix Design and Durability Strategy
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit complete concrete mix design including w/c ratio, durability class designation (per ACI 201.2R), chloride limits, air entrainment specification, and durability testing plan for Government approval prior to any concrete batch operations.

requirements.title: Marine Cement and Supplementary Cementitious Materials (SCM) Certifications
requirements.description: Submit mill certificates, test reports, and SDS for all cements (Type II/V minimum), fly ash, slag, or silica fume. All materials must meet ASTM C150 and durability requirements for marine exposure.

requirements.title: Compressive Strength Testing (Standard Cylinders)
requirements.description: Test cast cylinders at 7-day and 28-day intervals per ASTM C39 to verify strength development and durability mix performance.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 set per 100 cubic yards per pour (minimum 2 cylinders per set)
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Minimum 3500 psi at 7 days; minimum 4500 psi at 28 days

requirements.title: Rapid Chloride Penetration Test (RCPT)
requirements.description: Determine chloride ion penetration depth and charge passed per ASTM C1202 on 28-day cores to validate durability performance in marine exposure.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per 200 cubic yards (minimum 3 specimens)
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Maximum 2000 coulombs (LOW durability class per ACI 201.2R); target ≤1000 coulombs (VERY LOW)

requirements.title: Freeze-Thaw Durability (ASTM C666)
requirements.description: Evaluate concrete resistance to cycles of freezing and thawing with deicing salts, critical for tidal zone and splash zone elements at Naval Station Neverland.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per construction phase or per 500 cubic yards
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Relative durability factor ≥80% after 300 cycles; mass loss <5%

requirements.title: Concrete Placement and Air Entrainment Verification
requirements.description: QC shall witness fresh concrete placement, verify air content (target 6–8% for marine exposure), slump (4–5 inches), temperature (50–90°F), and consolidation in accordance with ACI 305.

requirements.title: Durability Test Summary Report
requirements.description: Contractor shall compile all RCPT, freeze-thaw, strength, and air content results into a final durability compliance report for Government acceptance upon substantial completion of concrete work.

requirements.title: Marine Concrete Compliance Certificate
requirements.description: Independent testing lab shall issue a marine concrete durability certification confirming all test results meet or exceed Section 03 31 29 requirements and ACI 201.2R marine environment standards.

sections.aiSummary: Section 03 45 33 governs the fabrication, delivery, and installation of precast and prestressed structural concrete members for the dry dock's primary structural frame, including deck planks, wall panels, and support columns. QC verification of mill certifications, dimensional accuracy, strand release procedures, and concrete strength are critical to ensure structural integrity and schedule compliance on this naval facility project.

requirements.title: Precast/Prestressed Concrete Plant Qualification & Quality Plan
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit evidence of plant ACI certification, quality control procedures, personnel qualifications, and a detailed QCP addressing prestress release methodology, strand tensioning verification, and concrete cure monitoring.

requirements.title: Shop Drawings – Precast Members & Prestressing Layout
requirements.description: Submit detailed shop drawings including member dimensions, prestressing strand pattern, end block details, lifting/handling points, and connections to cast-in-place elements with calculation support.

requirements.title: Concrete Compressive Strength – Prestress Release
requirements.description: Test concrete cylinder samples from each precast member batch to confirm minimum strength at time of prestress release.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per 50 CY per member type
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: minimum 3500 psi at release; 4500 psi at 28 days

requirements.title: Prestressing Strand Tensioning & Release Verification
requirements.description: Verify initial jacking stress, hold time, and gradual release procedure using load cells and strain gauges; document all data in real-time.
requirements.testFrequency: 100% of members (continuous monitoring)
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: jacking stress within ±3% of design; controlled release per ACI 423.7

requirements.title: Factory Inspection – Dimensional Verification & Surface Condition
requirements.description: Government QC witness dimension check (length, width, depth, skew) and visual inspection for cracks, spalls, or surface defects before shipment from plant.

requirements.title: Material Certifications – Prestressing Strand & Reinforcement
requirements.description: Provide mill test reports (MTRs) and certs for all prestressing strand (ASTM A416 Grade 270) and reinforcing steel, including tensile properties and chemical analysis.

requirements.title: Delivery & Receiving Inspection Report
requirements.description: Document condition of precast members upon arrival at Naval Station Neverland, including photographic evidence of any damage, dimensional spot-checks, and traceability to shop certifications.

requirements.title: Final Precast Member Release & Compliance Certificate
requirements.description: Contractor submits signed certificate confirming all QC tests passed, design requirements met, and members are cleared for installation per contract documents.

sections.aiSummary: Specification section 04 20 00 — UNIT MASONRY — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.
Specification section 04 23 00 — GLASS UNIT MASONRY WINDOWS — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

Section 05 05 23.16 governs all structural welding processes, welder qualifications, materials, and inspection procedures for the hybrid dry dock's primary steel frame and connection details. QC oversight ensures compliance with AWS D1.1 standards, proper documentation of welder certifications, and non-destructive testing (NDT) results to verify weld integrity in this critical load-bearing structure.

requirements.title: Welding Procedure Specifications (WPS) and Procedure Qualification Records (PQR)
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit all AWS-compliant WPS documents and supporting PQR test data for each welding process, material combination, and position used on the project. Government approval required before any structural welding begins.

requirements.title: Welder Qualification Certificates (AWS D1.1)
requirements.description: Contractor certifies current AWS D1.1 welder qualifications for all personnel performing structural welds. Certificates must be on-site and current before work; re-qualification required every 6 months if no welding performed.

requirements.title: Base Metal and Filler Material Inspection
requirements.description: QC shall verify mill test reports for all structural steel base materials and inspect incoming filler materials (electrodes, wire, flux) for damage, contamination, and proper storage conditions before use.

requirements.title: Visual Weld Inspection (VT) – In-Process
requirements.description: Government QC witness shall observe 100% of all primary load-bearing welds during and immediately after deposition to verify conformance to drawing details, bead profile, and freedom from surface defects per AWS D1.1 acceptance criteria.

requirements.title: Radiographic Inspection (RT) of Critical Welds
requirements.description: Contractor shall perform radiographic examination on 10% of all primary frame connection welds (minimum 1 per connection type) per ASTM E94 standards.
requirements.testFrequency: 10% of primary welds; minimum 1 per connection detail type
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: No linear indications exceeding 1/8 inch; no clusters of porosity per AWS D1.1 Table 6.1

requirements.title: Ultrasonic Testing (UT) of Subsurface Defects
requirements.description: High-stress butt welds on main girders and critical splice plates shall be examined by UT to detect internal voids, cracks, and lack of fusion not visible by RT.
requirements.testFrequency: 5% of butt welds on primary load paths
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: No indications greater than 1/16 inch in thickness; rejection if crack-like indications detected

requirements.title: Weld Inspection and Test Reports (Daily/Weekly Summary)
requirements.description: Contractor submits documented VT results, RT/UT reports, and any deficiency logs with corrective actions taken. Reports shall correlate to structural drawings and mark-ups showing weld locations and acceptance status.

requirements.title: Welder Health & Safety Documentation and OSHA Compliance Plan
requirements.description: Contractor submits confined-space entry procedures, hot-work permits, respiratory protection plan, and ventilation strategy specific to dry dock welding operations in enclosed compartments.

sections.aiSummary: Section 05 12 00 governs the fabrication, delivery, and installation of structural steel members for the TIER 3 hybrid dry dock, including columns, beams, bracing, and connections. QC verification ensures compliance with AISC standards, material certifications, dimensional tolerances, and welding quality to guarantee structural integrity and safety of the dock facility.

requirements.title: Structural Steel Fabrication Plan and Schedule
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed fabrication plan including mill sources, fabrication sequence, inspection schedule, and delivery timeline for all structural steel members. Plan must identify critical path items and long-lead components.

requirements.title: Shop Drawings for Structural Steel
requirements.description: Shop drawings shall include member sizing, connection details, welding symbols per AWS D1.1, bolt schedules, camber information, and dimensional tolerances for all primary and secondary steel members.

requirements.title: Mill Certificates and Material Certifications
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit mill test reports (MTRs) and Certificates of Compliance for all structural steel plates, shapes, and fasteners confirming ASTM A992 Grade 50 compliance and chemical composition.

requirements.title: Weld Procedure Specification (WPS) and Welder Qualification
requirements.description: Contractor shall conduct bend tests and tensile tests on welded coupons per AWS D1.1 Section 5 prior to production welding; all welders must pass qualification testing.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 WPS per connection type; 1 qualification per welder before dock work begins
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Bend test specimens show no cracks exceeding 1/8 inch; tensile strength ≥95% of base metal strength

requirements.title: Fabrication Inspection and Dimensional Verification
requirements.description: Government QC shall witness in-plant inspection of all primary structural members for dimensional accuracy, surface finish (wire brush minimum per SSPC-SP 3), and defect-free condition before shipment.

requirements.title: Bolted and Welded Connection Assembly
requirements.description: Government QC shall witness assembly and torquing of all bolted connections and visual inspection of welded connections prior to acceptance. Snug-tight torque values per AISC 360 shall be verified with calibrated wrenches.

requirements.title: Ultrasonic Testing (UT) of Welds
requirements.description: Contractor shall perform ultrasonic inspection on a sample of critical welds (primary load-bearing connections and splices) per ASME Section VIII or AWS D1.1 acceptance standards.
requirements.testStandard: ASME Section VIII / AWS D1.1 Section 6
requirements.testFrequency: 10% of production welds on primary members
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: No indications exceeding ¼ inch length; no laminations or lack of fusion

requirements.title: Final Structural Steel Completion Certificate
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit as-built documentation, inspection records, test reports, bolt torque logs, and final certification that all structural steel is complete and meets contract specifications.

sections.sectionTitle: COATING OF STEEL WATERFRONT STRUCTURES, ZERO VOC, SPLASH ZONE COATING (SZC)
sections.aiSummary: This section governs the selection, application, and QC of zero-VOC splash zone coatings (SZC) for steel waterfront structures in the hybrid dry dock at NSN, ensuring corrosion protection and environmental compliance in the harsh marine environment. Splash zone coatings experience cyclical wetting/drying and salt spray exposure, making surface prep, coating compatibility, and application technique critical to performance and durability.

requirements.title: Shop Drawings – Surface Prep & Coating Application Plan
requirements.description: Contractor submits detailed surface preparation procedures (SSPC-SP standards, grit size, blast pressure), coating application sequencing, environmental conditions (temperature, humidity, dew point), and touch-up/repair protocols for Government approval before blast/coat work begins.

requirements.title: Product Data & Zero-VOC Certification
requirements.description: Manufacturer technical data sheets, VOC certificates (≤0 g/L), pot life, dry times, surface prep requirements, recoat windows, and splash zone performance test results (ASTM B117 salt spray, ASTM D3359 adhesion) submitted for approval.

requirements.title: Coating Sample Approval – Color & Finish
requirements.description: QC holds physical 12×12 in. panels coated with approved primer and topcoat applied per spec conditions; Government inspects for gloss, color match, and surface uniformity before full-scale application authorization.

requirements.title: Surface Profile & Cleanliness Verification
requirements.description: After blast cleaning, QC measures surface profile depth (ASTM D4417, target 2.5–3.5 mils for SZC), verifies SSPC-PS blast standard compliance, and checks salt contamination (ASTM B117 salt fog readiness) at random structural sections.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per 500 sq ft blasted, minimum 3 per coat bay
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Profile 2.5–3.5 mils, SSPC-SP 6 or better, salt residue <5 µg/cm²

requirements.title: Primer Application Start – Environmental Verification
requirements.description: QC witnesses start of primer application; verifies ambient temperature 50–95°F, relative humidity <85%, dew point ≥5°F above steel surface, and spray pattern/technique compliance before contractor proceeds.

requirements.title: Wet Film Thickness & Dry Film Thickness (DFT)
requirements.description: During and after each coat application, QC measures wet film thickness with wet gauge and verifies dry film thickness after cure per coating schedule; topcoat SZC target 8–10 mils DFT on splash zone.
requirements.testFrequency: Minimum 10 spots per 1,000 sq ft per coat; daily during application
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Dry film thickness 8–10 mils topcoat, 4–6 mils primer; ≥90% of readings within range

requirements.title: Adhesion & Holiday Detection
requirements.description: After cure, QC performs cross-hatch adhesion (ASTM D3359) and electric holiday detection (ASTM D4787) on coated steel in splash zone to confirm no voids, pinholes, or delamination.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 adhesion test per 2,000 sq ft; holiday scan 100% of splash zone
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Adhesion ≥4B rating; zero holidays >1 mm diameter

requirements.title: Coating Inspection & Test Report
requirements.description: QC compiles daily logs (temp, humidity, DFT, defects, touch-ups) and submits final coating report with photos, test certificates, and as-built splash zone coating area summary for Government final approval and closeout.
