Section 21 12 00 governs the design, installation, testing, and commissioning of fire water systems serving the Tier 3 hybrid dry dock at Naval Station Neverland, including pump stations, distribution piping, hydrants, monitors, and automated suppression equipment. This section is critical for QC because fire protection systems must meet Navy and NFPA standards with zero tolerance for defects that could compromise life safety or asset protection during dry dock operations.

requirements.title: Fire Water System Design Report and Calculations
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit complete fire water system hydraulic design calculations, including pump selection, pressure requirements, flow rates for all demand scenarios, and compliance verification against NFPA 13, NFPA 14, and Navy Fire Protection standards.

requirements.title: Shop Drawings – Pump Stations and Riser Diagrams
requirements.description: Detailed isometric riser diagrams, pump station equipment layouts, control panel schematics, and interconnection details for all fire water supply sources and distribution network serving the dry dock basin.

requirements.title: Product Data – Pumps, Valves, and Hydrants
requirements.description: Manufacturer certified product data sheets, performance curves, and certification documents for all centrifugal pumps, check valves, control valves, pressure gauges, and fire hydrants to be installed in the system.

requirements.title: Hydrostatic Pressure Test – Distribution Piping
requirements.description: All fire water distribution piping shall be hydrostatically tested at 1.5 times the maximum working pressure for a minimum of 2 hours with zero visible leakage.
requirements.testFrequency: 100% of all piping segments; continuous observation by Government Inspector
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: No pressure drop exceeding 10 psi over 2-hour hold period; no visible leaks at connections or joints

requirements.title: Pump Performance Test and Flow Verification
requirements.description: Contractor shall conduct full-load pump test under simulated demand conditions to verify rated capacity, discharge pressure, and suction lift performance at all anticipated operating points.
requirements.testFrequency: One complete test per pump; witnessed by Government QC and Navy Engineering
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Pump shall deliver minimum design flow at minimum required discharge pressure; motor amperage shall not exceed nameplate rating

requirements.title: Fire Water System Acceptance Certificate
requirements.description: NAVFAC Government QC Manager shall issue final acceptance certificate only after all tests, inspections, and operational readiness checks are completed and documented; includes sign-off on compliance with all design standards and Navy directives.

requirements.title: As-Built Documentation and System Commissioning Report
requirements.description: Comprehensive as-built drawings, test reports summary, equipment serial numbers, maintenance logs, operator training records, and Navy facility turnover package documenting full system commissioning and compliance certification.

requirements.title: Fire Water System Operations and Maintenance Manual
requirements.description: Contractor shall prepare detailed O&M manual including system schematics, equipment operating procedures, routine maintenance schedules, emergency shutdown protocols, spare parts lists, and contact information for all original equipment manufacturers.

This section establishes cybersecurity requirements for all dock control systems (gate automation, vessel positioning, winch controls, and monitoring networks) integrated into the TIER 3 hybrid dry dock at Naval Station Neverland. QC must verify that all networked industrial control systems meet NIST SP 800-82 guidelines, DoD cybersecurity standards, and achieve air-gapped or segmented architecture to prevent unauthorized access and ensure operational continuity during critical naval vessel servicing operations.

requirements.title: Cybersecurity Design Plan
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit a comprehensive cybersecurity design plan documenting network topology, access controls, encryption protocols, and segmentation strategy for all dock control systems before any control system equipment procurement or installation.

requirements.title: PLC and SCADA System Shop Drawings
requirements.description: Shop drawings for all programmable logic controllers, human-machine interfaces, and supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) systems shall include hardware specifications, firmware versions, network interface details, and security-hardened configuration baseline.

requirements.title: Firewall and Network Access Control Product Data
requirements.description: Manufacturer technical data for industrial firewalls, intrusion detection/prevention systems, and VPN/jump-server appliances shall be submitted, including certifications, rule-set templates, and compliance documentation with NIST and DoD Cyber Security Technical Reference Architecture.

requirements.title: Cybersecurity Component Certificates and Compliance
requirements.description: Certificates of compliance from manufacturers confirming FIPS 140-2 validation (for encryption modules), Common Criteria EAL ratings, or DoD Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) status for control system hardware and software components.

requirements.title: Network Penetration Testing and Vulnerability Assessment
requirements.description: Independent third-party security firm shall conduct authenticated and unauthenticated penetration testing on the integrated dock control network to identify and remediate vulnerabilities before system handover.
requirements.testStandard: NIST SP 800-115 (Technical Security Testing), OWASP Testing Guide v4.1
requirements.testFrequency: 1 complete assessment during Factory Acceptance Test (FAT) and 1 during Site Acceptance Test (SAT)
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Zero critical vulnerabilities, zero high-risk findings without approved compensating controls; all medium-risk items documented with remediation timeline and Government acceptance

requirements.title: Firewall Rule-Set Validation and Access Control Testing
requirements.description: QC witness shall verify firewall configuration baseline, validate that all inbound/outbound rules comply with the approved cybersecurity design plan, and confirm that dock control systems are properly segmented from administrative and external networks.
requirements.testStandard: NIST SP 800-41 Rev. 1 (Guidelines on Firewalls and Firewall Policy)
requirements.testFrequency: 1 during system integration; 1 before SAT; 1 post-commissioning (90 days after NTP)
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: All firewall rules documented and approved; segmentation validated via network scanning; no unauthorized lateral movement detected between zones

requirements.title: Cybersecurity Baseline Configuration Report
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit signed baseline configuration report for all dock control systems including hardened OS configurations, disabled unnecessary services, patch levels, and security group memberships; shall be locked and versioned under change control.

requirements.title: Cybersecurity Incident Response and Monitoring Plan
requirements.description: Detailed plan describing 24/7 security monitoring, alerting thresholds, incident classification, escalation procedures, and coordination with Naval Station Neverland IT Security and Engineering duty officers during dock operations.

Section 26 42 13 governs the design, fabrication, installation, and testing of galvanic (sacrificial) anode cathodic protection (GACP) systems for steel structures and embedded metallic elements in the TIER 3 hybrid dry dock. This section ensures corrosion protection of submerged and tidal-zone structural steel, gate components, and penstock systems through proper anode material selection, spacing, attachment, and performance verification.

requirements.title: GACP System Design and Calculations
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed GACP design calculations including anode material type, weight, spacing, expected design life, and current output predictions based on seawater resistivity and structure surface area. Calculations must comply with NACE SP0387 and reference site-specific marine environment data.

requirements.title: Anode Material Certification and Mill Test Reports
requirements.description: Manufacturer certificates of compliance and mill test reports for all zinc or aluminum sacrificial anode material shall be submitted, confirming alloy composition, weight tolerance (±5%), and compliance with ASTM B418 (zinc) or ASTM B521 (aluminum).

requirements.title: Installation and Attachment Procedures
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed installation procedures covering anode attachment methods (mechanical fastening, brazing, or welding), spacing tolerances, electrical continuity verification, and surface preparation protocols for the dry dock structure and penstock.

requirements.title: Pre-Installation Electrical Continuity Testing
requirements.description: Government shall witness electrical resistance testing between anode attachment points and the protected structure. All connections shall exhibit resistance ≤0.1 ohm.
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Resistance ≤0.1 ohm per connection; no open circuits

requirements.title: Initial Potential Survey and Anode Performance Verification
requirements.description: After anode installation, contractor shall conduct initial seawater potential survey using half-cell electrode method to verify cathodic protection potential targets are met across all protected zones.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 baseline survey + 1 per 500 linear feet of structure
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Potential ≥ -850 mV (CSE) in immersion zone; ≥ -750 mV in tidal zone

requirements.title: Anode Attachment and Continuity Approval
requirements.description: Government must visually inspect and approve all anode attachment points, fastener integrity, and cable routing before any coating operations or water introduction to the dry dock basin.

requirements.title: System Performance and Monitoring Plan
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit a comprehensive GACP system monitoring and maintenance plan detailing annual potential surveys, anode life expectancy calculations, replacement procedures, and inspection schedules for the 25-year design life.

requirements.title: As-Built GACP System Certification
requirements.description: Contractor shall provide signed as-built certification documenting all anode locations, weights, attachment details, electrical continuity test results, and baseline potential survey data for Government records and future O&M.

sections.sectionTitle: CATHODIC PROTECTION SYSTEM FOR THE INTERIOR OF STEEL WATER TANKS
sections.aiSummary: Specification section 26 42 15 — CATHODIC PROTECTION SYSTEM FOR THE INTERIOR OF STEEL WATER TANKS — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

Section 26 42 17 governs the design, installation, testing, and commissioning of an impressed current cathodic protection (ICCP) system to protect steel structures and reinforced concrete in the hybrid dry dock from corrosion in the marine environment. QC must verify anode bed integrity, power supply output, potential surveys, and system performance prior to operational handover.

requirements.title: ICCP System Design and Calculations
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed design calculations including anode bed layout, impressed current distribution analysis, and cathodic protection criteria per NACE SP0169 for concrete and steel elements. Design must address seawater immersion environment and dry dock operational scenarios.

requirements.title: Rectifier and Control System Product Data
requirements.description: Manufacturer technical data sheets for DC power supply, automatic potential controller, and reference electrode system including output ratings, voltage/current ranges, and corrosion inhibitor injection capability.

requirements.title: Anode Material Certification
requirements.description: Manufacturer certificates of compliance for all anodes (mixed metal oxide or graphite) certifying composition, weight, and dimensional conformance to ASTM B418 or equivalent.

requirements.title: Pre-Installation Anode Bed Resistance Measurement
requirements.description: Verify installed anode bed resistance to seawater/concrete interface meets design criteria prior to energization. Measure resistance-to-earth at each anode location using established electrode methods.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per anode bed installation
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Anode bed resistance ≤ design specification; typically 0.5 to 2.0 ohms depending on geometry and environment

requirements.title: Initial Potential Survey (OFF State)
requirements.description: Conduct baseline cathodic potential measurements on all steel and reinforced concrete elements with ICCP system de-energized using copper/copper sulfate reference electrode.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 before system energization
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Document baseline potentials on survey map; record at minimum 30 locations per dry dock section

requirements.title: System Energization and Operating Potential Survey
requirements.description: Energize ICCP system at design current output and measure cathodic potentials across all protected surfaces at steady state (minimum 48 hours operation). Verify all test points achieve protection criteria.
requirements.testStandard: ASTM G71, NACE SP0169, NACE SP0495
requirements.testFrequency: 1 after system energization; repeat quarterly during first year
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Steel: -0.85 V to -1.05 V (Cu/CuSO₄); Concrete: -0.20 V minimum; Potential shift ≥ 0.30 V from baseline or absolute criteria achieved

requirements.title: ICCP System Commissioning Report
requirements.description: Comprehensive report documenting all installation inspections, resistance measurements, potential surveys, system parameter logs, and final certification that the ICCP system is operational and meets all design and performance criteria.

requirements.title: Operation, Maintenance, and Monitoring Manual
requirements.description: Complete O&M documentation including rectifier operating procedures, automatic controller settings, monthly monitoring schedules, troubleshooting guide, spare parts list, and quarterly potential survey protocol for facility personnel.

Section 31 23 00.00 20 governs excavation, dewatering, and fill placement for the hybrid dry dock basin and supporting infrastructure at Naval Station Neverland, including site grading, material handling, and compaction verification. Proper QC of these operations is critical to ensure structural support capacity, drainage performance, and long-term stability of the 350-foot dry dock structure and ancillary facilities.

requirements.title: Excavation and Fill Plan
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed excavation, dewatering, and fill placement plan including sequencing, equipment schedule, material sources, and compaction methodology for the dry dock basin and surrounding grade work.

requirements.title: Soil and Borrow Material Certification
requirements.description: Provide laboratory certifications and classification reports (USCS) for all excavated and imported fill materials, including bearing capacity and compaction test data from source location.

requirements.title: Field Density Testing – Compacted Fill
requirements.description: Verify compaction of all placed fill material meets 95% Standard Proctor dry density in 150-foot grid pattern and at material changes.
requirements.testStandard: ASTM D1556 (Sand Cone) or ASTM D2922 (Nuclear Gauge)
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per 5,000 cubic yards of fill placed
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Minimum 95% Standard Proctor density; moisture within ±2% of optimum

requirements.title: Subgrade Bearing Ratio Testing
requirements.description: California Bearing Ratio (CBR) testing on prepared subgrade to confirm structural support capacity for dry dock foundation loading.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per grid cell (minimum 3 locations within basin footprint)
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Minimum CBR of 8% at basin invert; minimum CBR of 6% at surrounding grade areas

requirements.title: Excavation to Subgrade Elevation
requirements.description: Government representative must observe and approve final excavation depth and confirmation that native subgrade elevations match design plans before fill placement begins.

requirements.title: Dewatering System Approval
requirements.description: Dewatering system installation and discharge point permits must be verified and approved by Government and environmental compliance officer before excavation of below-water-table materials.

requirements.title: Daily Excavation and Fill Log
requirements.description: Contractor shall maintain daily records of excavation volumes, fill material placement, compaction certification, moisture readings, and any deviations from plan with photographic documentation.

requirements.title: Final Compaction and Grade Certification
requirements.description: Upon completion of all fill and grading operations, Contractor's QC engineer shall certify that all work meets specification requirements, test results are filed, and final grades match design.

Section 31 62 13.20 governs the design, manufacturing, delivery, and installation quality of precast/prestressed concrete piles for the TIER 3 hybrid dry dock foundation system at Naval Station Neverland. QC oversight ensures pile capacity, dimensional accuracy, prestress force verification, and defect-free delivery to prevent structural integrity failures and schedule delays.

requirements.title: Pile Design and Prestress Shop Drawings
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed shop drawings showing pile reinforcement layout, prestress tendon profile and force diagram, strand pattern, lift and handling points, and casting procedure. Government reviews for compliance with contract documents and structural design intent.

requirements.title: Pile Manufacturer Certifications and Product Data
requirements.description: Manufacturer shall provide mill certifications for concrete mix design, strand material certifications (ASTM A416), strength test reports, and quality control procedures. Includes casting yard location approval and quality assurance plan.

requirements.title: Concrete Compressive Strength – Casting Lot Samples
requirements.description: Cylinders cast from each pile concrete batch to verify design strength at 7 and 28 days. Minimum three cylinders per test date; results submitted before pile acceptance.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 set per 50 cubic yards of concrete
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Minimum 5000 psi at 28 days; no individual result more than 10% below design strength

requirements.title: Prestress Force Verification – Strand Tensioning
requirements.description: Load cell or calibrated dynamometer readings verify prestress force applied to each pile strand bundle before concrete placement. Contractor records and submits jacking logs for Government review.
requirements.testFrequency: 100% of all piles; recorded per tensioning event
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Achieved force within 95–105% of design prestress force; variation between strands ≤ 5%

requirements.title: Pile Dimensional and Defect Inspection
requirements.description: QC Manager and Government representative inspect each finished pile for length, diameter, camber, surface defects (cracks >0.01 in., spalling, exposed aggregate), reinforcement cover, and proper lift point installation before shipment from casting yard.
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Dimensions within ±0.5 in.; no structural cracks; lift points per design; surface finish per Section 31 62 13.20 paragraph 3.5

requirements.title: Pile Delivery and Unloading
requirements.description: Government witness pile delivery and unloading at dock storage area. Inspector verifies pile identification markings, secures proper storage on bearing blocks to prevent differential settlement, and documents condition upon arrival.
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Piles stored horizontally on supports ≥ 2 ft from ends; no visible new damage; proper stenciled ID visible

requirements.title: Pile Installation Records and Load Test Summary
requirements.description: Contractor submits final summary report documenting all pile driving records (blow counts, set, driving logs), any pile damage or repair, and if required, dynamic load test results per ASTM D4945. Report certifies all piles installed and verified to design capacity.

requirements.title: Manufacturer Mill Test Report and Compliance Certificate
requirements.description: Third-party testing lab or manufacturer provides signed mill test certificates for concrete strength, prestressing strand material certifications, and final compliance statement confirming all piles meet contract specifications.

sections.aiSummary: Section 31 62 16.16 governs the procurement, fabrication, delivery, and installation of structural steel H-piles for the TIER 3 hybrid dry dock at Naval Station Neverland. This specification ensures H-piles meet ASTM A588 Grade 50 requirements, are properly certified, inspected, and installed to design specifications critical for the dock's structural integrity and load-bearing capacity.

requirements.title: H-Pile Shop Drawings and Fabrication Details
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed shop drawings showing pile dimensions, section properties, material grades, welded details, surface treatment specifications, and installation methodology for Government review and approval prior to fabrication.

requirements.title: Material Certification and Mill Test Reports
requirements.description: Manufacturer shall provide certified mill test reports (MTRs) for all structural steel H-piles verifying chemical composition and mechanical properties per ASTM A588 Grade 50, including tensile strength, yield strength, and elongation.

requirements.title: Tensile Testing of H-Pile Material
requirements.description: Independent testing laboratory shall perform tensile tests on representative samples from each heat lot of steel H-piles per ASTM A370 standards.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per heat lot (minimum 1 per 25 tons)
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Yield strength ≥50 ksi; Tensile strength 70–100 ksi; Elongation ≥21% (in 8 inches)

requirements.title: H-Pile Surface Preparation and Coating Inspection
requirements.description: Government QC shall witness surface cleaning (SSPC-SP6 Commercial Steel Blast Cleaning) and application of epoxy coating system prior to shipment to site.

requirements.title: H-Pile Delivery and On-Site Inspection
requirements.description: All H-piles shall be inspected upon delivery for dimensional accuracy, surface defects, coating damage, and proper identification/traceability before acceptance into the construction site.

requirements.title: Ultrasonic Thickness Testing of H-Pile Web and Flanges
requirements.description: QC shall perform ultrasonic thickness measurements on random H-piles post-delivery to verify mill tolerance compliance and detect corrosion or material loss.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per 10 piles or 1 per day
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Thickness within ±0.125 inch of nominal design thickness; no areas <95% nominal

requirements.title: H-Pile Installation Verification and Alignment
requirements.description: Prior to final setting, Government shall verify pile plumbness (±0.5% of length), cut lengths, embedment depth, and cap seating to ensure structural compliance with design drawings.

requirements.title: Fabricator Quality Assurance and Traceability Certificate
requirements.description: Fabricator shall provide a signed certificate documenting all piles' serial numbers, heat lot traceability, MTR references, surface preparation records, and quality control test results.

sections.aiSummary: Section 31 63 29 governs the design, materials, installation, inspection, and testing of drilled reinforced concrete piers and shafts that serve as primary foundation elements for the hybrid dry dock structure. QC enforcement of drilling tolerances, reinforcement placement, concrete quality, and load-bearing verification is critical to ensure structural integrity and naval vessel support capability.

requirements.title: Drilled Pier Design and Installation Plan
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed design calculations, boring logs, pier layout drawings, drilling methodology, casing specifications, and dewatering procedures for Government review and approval prior to pier drilling operations.

requirements.title: Reinforcement Shop Drawings
requirements.description: Shop drawings for reinforcement cages, cage assembly procedures, lifting/handling plans, and splice details shall be submitted and approved by Government before cage fabrication.

requirements.title: Drilled Shaft Inspection Before Concrete Placement
requirements.description: Government QC must inspect and approve shaft cleanliness, final diameter verification, reinforcement cage positioning, depth measurement, and absence of slough before concrete tremie operations commence.

requirements.title: Concrete Compressive Strength
requirements.description: Concrete samples from each pier pour shall be tested for compressive strength per ASTM C39 at 7 and 28 days to verify design strength.
requirements.testFrequency: 2 cylinders per 50 cubic yards, minimum 1 per pier
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Minimum 4000 psi at 28 days; no single result below 90% of specified strength

requirements.title: Concrete Slump and Air Content
requirements.description: Slump and air content shall be measured on concrete delivered to each pier tremie pour to ensure workability and quality consistency.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 slump test and 1 air test per pier pour
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Slump 4–6 inches; air content 4–8%

requirements.title: Reinforcement Cage Installation and Positioning
requirements.description: Government QC shall witness lowering, centering, and securing of reinforcement cages within each drilled shaft to verify proper depth, lateral positioning, and verticality within specified tolerances.

requirements.title: Pier Load Test Report (if required)
requirements.description: If specified in contract, Contractor shall submit results of static or dynamic load testing on selected piers, including load-displacement curves and bearing capacity verification.

requirements.title: Drilled Pier As-Built Certification
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit final as-built report documenting actual pier depths, diameters, concrete volumes, reinforcement details, and any deviations from design with Government-approved corrective actions.

sections.sectionTitle: SEALING OF JOINTS IN RIGID PAVEMENT
sections.aiSummary: Specification section 32 01 19.61 — SEALING OF JOINTS IN RIGID PAVEMENT — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.
Specification section 32 11 20 — BASE COURSE FOR RIGID PAVING — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.
Specification section 32 11 23 — AGGREGATE BASE COURSES — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.
Specification section 32 12 16.16 — ROAD-MIX ASPHALT PAVING — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

sections.sectionTitle: CONCRETE PAVING FOR HEAVY DUTY PAVEMENTS
sections.aiSummary: Specification section 32 13 14.13 — CONCRETE PAVING FOR HEAVY DUTY PAVEMENTS — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

Specification section 32 13 73.19 — COMPRESSION CONCRETE PAVING JOINT SEALANT — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.
Specification section 32 17 23 — PAVEMENT MARKINGS — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.
Specification section 32 31 13 — CHAIN LINK FENCES AND GATES — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

sections.sectionTitle: SINGLE WALL ABOVEGROUND FIXED ROOF STEEL WATER STORAGE TANK
sections.aiSummary: Specification section 33 56 21.17 — SINGLE WALL ABOVEGROUND FIXED ROOF STEEL WATER STORAGE TANK — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.
Specification section 33 56 53 — COMPRESSED GASES STORAGE TANKS — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.
Specification section 33 71 01 — OVERHEAD TRANSMISSION AND DISTRIBUTION — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.
Specification section 33 71 02 — UNDERGROUND ELECTRICAL DISTRIBUTION — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

Specification section 33 81 27 — DOCKSIDE TELECOMMUNICATIONS DISTRIBUTION SYSTEMS — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

sections.sectionTitle: WELDING CRANE AND RAILROAD RAIL — THERMITE METHOD
sections.aiSummary: Specification section 34 11 19.00 20 — WELDING CRANE AND RAILROAD RAIL — THERMITE METHOD — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.
Specification section 35 20 14 — WEIRS — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

Section 35 20 23 governs the removal, handling, and disposal of material from the hybrid dry dock basin and approach channels at Naval Station Neverland to achieve design depths and clearances. Quality control verification ensures dredged material meets environmental, safety, and disposal standards while maintaining structural integrity of adjacent facilities.

requirements.title: Dredging Plan and Sequencing
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed dredging plan including equipment specifications, disposal site location, environmental controls, and construction sequencing. Plan must address proximity to existing berthing structures and demonstrate protection of utilities.

requirements.title: Dredge Material Characterization Data
requirements.description: Laboratory analysis of representative soil samples from dredge area including grain size distribution, contamination screening per EPA protocols, and classification per USACE soil taxonomy.

requirements.title: Bathymetric Survey—Pre-Dredging Baseline
requirements.description: Licensed surveyor shall establish baseline water depth and bottom profile using dual-frequency sonar. Survey grid spacing shall not exceed 50 feet in main basin.
requirements.testFrequency: Once before dredging begins
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Survey accuracy ±0.2 feet vertical; GPS horizontal accuracy ±0.5 feet

requirements.title: Dredging Equipment Mobilization and Setup
requirements.description: Government shall witness positioning, calibration, and initial operation of dredge, pump systems, and overflow containment. Verify all environmental protection measures are in place.

requirements.title: Bathymetric Survey—Progress and Final
requirements.description: Licensed surveyor shall conduct progress surveys at 25% and 50% basin depth removal, and final as-dredged survey upon completion. Document deviation from design dredge lines.
requirements.testFrequency: 3 surveys during dredging; final survey mandatory
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Design depth achieved within ±0.3 feet; no overdredging exceeding 0.5 feet

requirements.title: Dredged Material Disposal Documentation
requirements.description: Contractor shall provide manifests and disposal site certifications confirming proper handling and placement of all dredged material per environmental permits and USACE requirements.

requirements.title: Dredging Completion Certificate
requirements.description: Licensed dredging contractor certifies all dredged material removed, design depths achieved, and site restored per plans. Includes references to all bathymetric surveys and as-built documentation.

requirements.title: Final Inspection and Acceptance of Dredged Basin
requirements.description: Government shall conduct final walkdown and review all survey data, disposal records, and photographs. Verify no debris, obstructions, or hazardous material remains in dredge area.

sections.sectionTitle: STONE, CHANNEL, SHORELINE/COASTAL PROTECTION FOR STRUCTURES
sections.aiSummary: Section 35 31 19 governs the procurement, testing, and installation of stone channel protection materials (armor stone, underlayer stone, and filter stone) used to protect the hybrid dry dock structure from wave action, erosion, and hydrodynamic forces in the Naval Station Neverland coastal environment. QC oversight ensures stone gradation, durability, placement density, and geometric tolerances meet design specifications to prevent premature failure of the shoreline/coastal protection system.

requirements.title: Quarry Certification and Stone Source Documentation
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit quarry certifications, geological reports, and source documentation for all armor stone, underlayer stone, and filter stone prior to procurement. Documentation must include stone type, origin, durability testing results, and proposed extraction/processing methods.

requirements.title: Stone Gradation Analysis and Mill Certificates
requirements.description: Submit mill test reports and gradation analysis (sieve curves) for each stone size classification, including armor stone (primary and secondary), underlayer stone, and filter stone. Reports must certify compliance with design gradation curves and D50 specifications.

requirements.title: Los Angeles Abrasion Test – Armor Stone
requirements.description: Perform Los Angeles abrasion testing on representative armor stone samples to verify durability and resistance to mechanical degradation during handling, placement, and service.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per 500 tons of armor stone
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: LA Abrasion loss ≤ 40% for primary armor stone; ≤ 45% for secondary armor stone

requirements.title: Soundness Test – All Stone Classifications
requirements.description: Conduct sodium sulfate soundness testing on armor stone, underlayer stone, and filter stone to evaluate resistance to freeze-thaw weathering and salt spray conditions at Naval Station Neverland.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per 750 tons per stone classification
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Mass loss ≤ 12% after 5 cycles of sodium sulfate exposure

requirements.title: Stone Delivery and Gradation Verification
requirements.description: Government QC shall witness receipt of all stone deliveries at the project site, verify truck manifests, and conduct field gradation sampling to confirm conformance to mill test reports and design specifications before placement approval.

requirements.title: Placement Density and Compaction Verification
requirements.description: Stone placement in the channel protection zone is a hold point. Contractor must demonstrate that placed stone achieves minimum unit weight density and proper grading through in-situ density testing and visual inspection before proceeding to next lift or adjacent areas.

requirements.title: In-Situ Stone Density and Settlement Monitoring
requirements.description: Perform nuclear density testing and elevation surveys on placed stone to verify compaction and settlement rates comply with design tolerances and prevent differential settlement that could damage dry dock structure.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per 250 CY placed; elevation surveys every 500 CY
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Minimum 95% of Standard Proctor density; settlement ≤ 2 inches per 10 feet of fill over 30 days

requirements.title: Final Stone Protection System Compliance Report
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit a comprehensive closeout report documenting all stone testing results, placement records, density verification, as-built elevations, and photographic evidence of completed channel and shoreline protection installation with certification of compliance to Section 35 31 19.

sections.aiSummary: Specification section 35 32 00 — LEVELING COURSE — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

Specification 35 45 01 governs the procurement, installation, testing, and acceptance of vertical turbine pumps and mixed-flow impeller-type units for the hybrid dry dock dewatering and circulation systems. QC oversight ensures pump performance meets design head/capacity requirements, mechanical integrity is verified through factory and field testing, and all equipment is properly certified before operational turnover.

requirements.title: Pump Shop Drawings and Design Data
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed shop drawings including impeller design, discharge head configuration, column pipe sizing, bearing specifications, and motor coupling details. Drawings must reference design head, capacity at design point, and performance curve certification.

requirements.title: Pump Manufacturer Product Data and Certifications
requirements.description: Manufacturer shall provide certified performance curves, material certifications (including ASTM grades for impeller and bowl materials), dimensional drawings, and nameplate data. All submittals must confirm NAVFAC hybrid dock compatibility requirements.

requirements.title: Factory Acceptance Test (FAT) – Pump Performance
requirements.description: Each pump shall undergo factory testing per ANSI/ASME B29.1 to verify head, capacity, efficiency, and vibration at design and off-design points. Test report must include pump curve validation, power draw confirmation, and bearing temperature monitoring.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per pump unit
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Head ±3% of design; capacity ±5%; efficiency ≥85% at design point; vibration ≤0.15 in/s peak

requirements.title: Field Acceptance Test (FAT) – Installation Verification
requirements.description: After installation, pump shall be tested at design flow and head with measurement of discharge pressure, input power, and vibration. Baseline data shall be recorded for condition monitoring during hybrid dock commissioning.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per installed pump
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Performance within 5% of FAT data; vibration <0.20 in/s peak; no audible cavitation

requirements.title: Material and Welds Certification – Pump Column and Discharge Head
requirements.description: Manufacturer shall provide certified mill test reports for all pressure-containing materials and documentation of weld inspection (visual, radiographic or UT per ASME Section VIII) for column and discharge head assemblies.

requirements.title: Manufacturer Operation, Maintenance, and Spare Parts Manual
requirements.description: Contractor shall obtain and submit O&M manual covering startup procedures, routine maintenance intervals, troubleshooting, bearing lubrication, impeller inspection criteria, and recommended spare parts list with lead times.

requirements.title: Pump Installation and Alignment Plan
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit method statement detailing pump installation sequence, motor-to-pump coupling alignment tolerance (±0.05 in. runout), column pipe thread/mechanical joint assembly, grouting or anchoring procedures, and noise/vibration isolation measures per NAVFAC hybrid dock design standards.

requirements.title: Vibration and Noise Baseline Report
requirements.description: Following field FAT, contractor shall measure and document baseline vibration (acceleration, velocity, displacement) at pump bearings and discharge in three orthogonal axes, plus sound level at 1 meter. Report establishes acceptance band for operational condition monitoring.

sections.aiSummary: Specification section 35 45 04.00 10 — SUBMERSIBLE PUMP, CENTRIFUGAL TYPE — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

Section 35 59 13.13 governs the design, manufacture, delivery, and installation of prestressed concrete fender piling for the Tier 3 hybrid dry dock at Naval Station Neverland. QC oversight ensures piles meet structural capacity, durability, and corrosion-resistance requirements critical to protecting the dock structure and berthing vessels.

requirements.title: Prestressed Concrete Pile Design & Manufacturing Plans
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed shop drawings showing pile geometry, prestressing strand pattern, tendon spacing, concrete mix design, and manufacturing procedures. Government must approve before pile fabrication begins.

requirements.title: Prestressing Steel & Concrete Material Certificates
requirements.description: Contractor certifies mill test reports for prestressing strand (ASTM A416), reinforcing steel, and concrete mix design with admixtures. Certificates must be on file before material delivery to site.

requirements.title: Concrete Compressive Strength Testing
requirements.description: Cylinder tests from each daily pour batch to verify 28-day strength meets design requirement.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per 100 cubic yards, minimum 2 per day
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Minimum 5000 psi at 28 days; no single test below 90% of design strength

requirements.title: Prestressing Force Verification (Strand Tensioning)
requirements.description: Measure jacking force and monitor elongation during stressing to confirm strand tension meets design loads per ASTM A416.
requirements.testFrequency: 100% of all strand groups per pile
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Jacking stress 0.75 fpu; elongation within ±6% of theoretical; documented on tension cards

requirements.title: Pile Transfer & Handling Inspection
requirements.description: QC witness lifting, curing, and transfer operations to confirm no damage, cracking, or mishandling of cured piles before shipment to Naval Station Neverland.

requirements.title: Pile Delivery & Dimensional Verification
requirements.description: Upon delivery to site, Government shall verify pile dimensions, surface condition, prestress alignment, and absence of cracks or spalls. Any nonconformance must be documented and approved for acceptance or rejection.

requirements.title: Installation & Load-Out Record
requirements.description: Contractor submits daily installation logs documenting pile placement sequence, driving records, set elevation, and any splicing or repairs performed during installation.

requirements.title: Mill Certificates & Compliance Affidavit
requirements.description: Contractor provides manufacturer's affidavit certifying all materials and processes conform to specification, ACI 318, and applicable ASTM standards. Required as final closeout document.

sections.aiSummary: Specification section 35 59 13.16 — EXTRUDED AND MOLDED MARINE FENDERS — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.
Specification section 35 59 30 — CAPSTANS AND ROLLER CHOCKS — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.
Specification section 40 05 13 — PIPELINES, LIQUID PROCESS PIPING — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.
Specification section 40 05 13.96 — WELDING PROCESS PIPING — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

Section 40 60 00 establishes quality control procedures, process monitoring, and documentation requirements for all construction phases of the TIER 3 hybrid dry dock at Naval Station Neverland. This section ensures systematic inspection, testing, and verification of materials, workmanship, and compliance with design specifications before, during, and after installation to maintain structural integrity and operational readiness.

requirements.title: Quality Control Plan (QCP)
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit a comprehensive QCP detailing inspection frequencies, testing protocols, documentation procedures, and corrective action processes for all dry dock construction phases including concrete, structural steel, and mechanical systems.

requirements.title: Concrete Compressive Strength Testing
requirements.description: Cylinder compression tests on concrete samples from dock floor, caisson, and wall pours to verify design strength and cure development.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 set per 100 cubic yards or per day, whichever is more frequent
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Minimum 90% of specified 28-day strength by test day; no single result below 85%

requirements.title: Reinforcing Steel Bond and Placement Verification
requirements.description: Covermeter surveys and visual inspection of rebar placement, spacing, and clearances prior to concrete placement in critical structural elements.
requirements.testFrequency: 100% coverage for critical load-bearing areas; minimum 3 points per 500 SF
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Concrete cover ±0.5 inch of design; spacing tolerance ±1 inch; no displacement during placement

requirements.title: Structural Steel Welding Inspections
requirements.description: Government QC witness of all structural steel welds in dock gates, caisson connections, and load-bearing frames; includes pre-weld, in-process, and post-weld inspections.

requirements.title: Ultrasonic Testing (UT) of Critical Welds
requirements.description: Non-destructive testing of structural steel welds at gates, caisson ring, and primary load paths to detect defects and ensure weld integrity.
requirements.testStandard: ASTM E494; AWS D1.1 acceptance criteria
requirements.testFrequency: 100% of primary welds; 25% of secondary welds
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: No indications exceeding 1/8 inch length; no linear indications >1/16 inch

requirements.title: Weekly Quality Control Reports
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit weekly reports documenting all inspections, tests performed, corrective actions, and compliance status with the QCP for Government review and record.

requirements.title: Material Certification and Mill Test Reports
requirements.description: Supplier certifications for concrete admixtures, structural steel mill certs, mechanical equipment ratings, and hydraulic system fluid analysis results must be submitted and approved before material incorporation.

requirements.title: Dry Dock Dewatering and Operational Readiness Review
requirements.description: Final Government QC inspection and approval of all process systems (pumping, drainage, power, lighting, safety systems) prior to initial dewatering operations.

sections.sectionTitle: ELECTRICAL SUPERVISORY CONTROL AND DATA ACQUISITION SYSTEM NEW FIELD EQUIPMENT
sections.aiSummary: Section 40 94 10 governs the design, installation, testing, and commissioning of new electrical Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) field equipment for the Tier 3 hybrid dry dock, including RTUs, sensors, transducers, and associated wiring infrastructure. QC oversight ensures compliance with naval standards, proper equipment integration, functional testing, and handover documentation to guarantee operational readiness of all monitoring and control systems.

requirements.title: SCADA Equipment Shop Drawings and System Architecture
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed shop drawings showing all field RTUs, sensor locations, wiring diagrams, terminations, grounding details, and system block diagrams illustrating integration with the central control station. Drawings must comply with IEEE 1379 and NFPA 70.

requirements.title: SCADA Field Equipment Product Data and Certifications
requirements.description: Submit manufacturer datasheets, technical specifications, calibration certificates, and material certifications for all RTUs, analog/digital sensors, transducers, and transmitters. All equipment must meet MIL-HDBK-418 environmental specifications for shipboard/naval facility service.

requirements.title: SCADA Installation and Commissioning Procedures
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed installation procedures, factory startup instructions, loop-checking protocols, and commissioning test plans. Include O&M manual excerpts, software load documentation, and cybersecurity hardening procedures per Navy cybersecurity policy.

requirements.title: Field Equipment Installation Inspection
requirements.description: All SCADA field sensors, RTUs, and wiring shall be inspected and approved by Government QC prior to energization or signal connection. Verify proper mounting, cable routing, grounding continuity, and label compliance.

requirements.title: Sensor Calibration and Loop Testing
requirements.description: Each analog sensor and transmitter shall undergo factory calibration verification and in-situ loop testing to confirm signal accuracy and repeatability across the operating range.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per sensor type; minimum 10% population random verification
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: ±0.5% accuracy; loop response time ≤2 seconds; repeatability ±0.25%

requirements.title: RTU Functional and Communication Testing
requirements.description: Each RTU shall be functionally tested for proper signal input/output, data logging, alarm logic, and bidirectional communication with the central SCADA host. Network latency and data integrity must be verified.
requirements.testFrequency: 100% of RTUs before commissioning
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: All I/O functional; network latency <500 ms; zero data packet loss over 1-hour test; all alarms trigger within specification

requirements.title: SCADA System Commissioning Certificate and Test Report
requirements.description: Upon successful completion of all factory acceptance tests and site commissioning, Contractor shall provide a signed certificate of compliance, final test reports, and as-built documentation confirming the SCADA field equipment is ready for operational handover and training.

requirements.title: SCADA System Performance and Cybersecurity Validation Report
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit a comprehensive post-commissioning report documenting system performance metrics, cybersecurity vulnerability assessments, penetration test results, and recommendations for ongoing maintenance and monitoring per Navy cybersecurity standards.

sections.sectionTitle: BRIDGE CRANES, OVERHEAD ELECTRIC, TOP RUNNING
sections.aiSummary: Section 41 22 13.14 governs the design, fabrication, installation, and testing of top-running electric overhead bridge cranes for the hybrid dry dock facility. QC oversight ensures structural integrity, electrical safety, load capacity certification, and operational readiness for naval vessel maintenance operations.

requirements.title: Bridge Crane Shop Drawings and Design Calculations
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed shop drawings including structural frame design, trolley and hoist assembly layouts, electrical single-line diagrams, load chart calculations, and foundation design per CMAA B-series standards. Design calculations must demonstrate load capacity at 125% of rated capacity.

requirements.title: Manufacturer Product Data and Certifications
requirements.description: Hoist manufacturer shall provide certified product data including load test certificates, wire rope certification, brake performance data, and motor specifications. All components must meet ASME B30.2 and electrical equipment must comply with NEMA MG-1.

requirements.title: Bridge Crane Structural Load Test
requirements.description: Perform proof load test at 125% of rated capacity with dial gauges monitoring deflection at mid-span and reactions at end trucks. Test shall be held for minimum 15 minutes with no permanent set.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per crane installation
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Deflection not to exceed L/600; no permanent deformation; all connections tight after load removal

requirements.title: Electrical System Insulation and Ground Continuity Test
requirements.description: Megohm resistance test on all motor windings and control circuit wiring; ground continuity test on structural frame bonding and pendant controls. Minimum 5 MΩ phase-to-ground and phase-to-phase.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 prior to energization
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: ≥5 MΩ all phases; ground resistance <0.1 Ω; no short circuits detected

requirements.title: Hoist and Trolley Brake Performance Test
requirements.description: Verify spring-set brakes hold rated load on incline (minimum 10°) for 5 minutes without slippage. Test both loaded and unloaded conditions.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per hoist assembly pre-installation, then annually
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Zero load drift; brake lining wear within limits; consistent brake torque ±10%

requirements.title: Factory Acceptance Test (FAT) Certificate
requirements.description: Manufacturer shall issue FAT certificate documenting successful completion of all shop load tests, electrical tests, and functional trials before shipment. Certificate must be signed by authorized factory representative.

requirements.title: Operation, Maintenance, and Inspection Manual
requirements.description: Contractor shall provide complete O&M manual including safe working load procedures, daily/weekly/monthly inspection checklists, lubrication schedules, brake adjustment procedures, and emergency stop protocols in compliance with ASME B30.2.

requirements.title: Installation Alignment and Runout Verification Report
requirements.description: Contractor shall document laser alignment of crane rails (±1/4 inch over 100 feet), trolley wheel runout, end truck squaring, and rope equalizer tension. Report must include as-built survey data and corrective actions taken.

sections.aiSummary: Section 41 22 13.33 governs the fabrication, delivery, installation, and alignment of structural steel portal crane track systems for the hybrid dry dock, including rail fastening, leveling, and functional testing. Proper track installation is critical to safe crane operations and load transfer to the dock structure during vessel handling and maintenance.

requirements.title: Portal Crane Track Shop Drawings
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed shop drawings showing track beam cross-sections, rail profiles, fastener details, expansion joint locations, and connection details to supporting structure. Drawings must include dimensional tolerances and deflection calculations.

requirements.title: Rail and Fastener Product Data
requirements.description: Submit manufacturer certifications and material test reports for crane rails (ASTM A1 or equivalent), tie plates, clips, bolts, and grout materials, including chemical composition and mechanical properties.

requirements.title: Track Installation and Alignment Procedure
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed installation procedure including sequencing, field welding (if applicable), bolt torque specifications, grouting methods, and laser-transit alignment methodology with target tolerances (±1/8 inch over 100 feet).

requirements.title: Track Structural Concrete Compressive Strength
requirements.description: Core sampling and compression testing of track support concrete to verify strength development before rail installation load.
requirements.testFrequency: 3 cores per 500 CY of concrete in track bed
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Minimum 90% of design strength (3,600 psi if 4,000 psi design)

requirements.title: Track Alignment Verification Survey
requirements.description: Government QC shall verify rail alignment, levelness, and gauge width using certified laser transit and precision level before fastener installation. All measurements documented with as-built survey.
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Track elevation ±1/8 inch over 100 feet; gauge width ±1/16 inch; cross-level ±1/8 inch over 50 feet

requirements.title: Track Bolt Torque Installation
requirements.description: QC witness contractor torque-wrench application of track fasteners to manufacturer-specified values, with calibrated tools and documentation per bolt location.
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: All bolts torqued to ±10% of specification; calibration certificates current

requirements.title: Portal Crane Test Load and Functional Inspection
requirements.description: Full operational test of portal crane on installed track at 125% design load, with measurement of track deflection, bolt preload retention, and rail wear.
requirements.testFrequency: Once upon installation completion
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Deflection ≤ 1/500 of span; no permanent set; no bolt loosening; rail undamaged

requirements.title: Track Installation Final Inspection Report
requirements.description: QC generates comprehensive final report documenting all surveys, tests, witness points, material certifications, and as-built conditions with photographs and sign-off.

sections.aiSummary: Specification section 46 21 00 — ROTARY LOBE PUMPS — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

SWBS 000 establishes administrative and quality control governance for all caisson construction activities at the hybrid dry dock, including submittals, inspections, testing protocols, and documentation requirements. This section is critical for QC because it defines the contractual framework, approval workflows, and hold/witness points that prevent non-conforming work from proceeding and ensures Government visibility at critical junctures.

requirements.title: Caisson Construction Quality Plan
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit a comprehensive Quality Plan covering caisson fabrication, installation, dewatering, and shoring operations. Plan must include inspection schedules, personnel qualifications, hold point protocols, and non-conformance procedures.

requirements.title: Caisson Shop Drawings and Fabrication Details
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed shop drawings for caisson components including wall reinforcement, internal bracing, bulkhead penetrations, and dewatering sump locations with design calculations.

requirements.title: Concrete Compressive Strength Testing—Caisson Walls
requirements.description: Cylinders cast from caisson wall concrete shall be tested for compressive strength at 7 days and 28 days per ASTM C39.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per 100 cubic yards, minimum 2 per pour
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Minimum 3500 psi at 7 days; 4500 psi at 28 days

requirements.title: Concrete Slump and Air Content—Caisson Pour
requirements.description: Slump and air content testing shall be performed on fresh caisson concrete at the point of placement to ensure workability and proper consolidation.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per caisson pour event or every 4 hours
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Slump 4–6 inches; air content 5–8%

requirements.title: Caisson Dewatering and Drying Certification
requirements.description: Before any interior construction work or coating application, the caisson shall be fully dewatered, inspected for leaks, and certified dry by the Contractor with Government witness.

requirements.title: Caisson Inspection and Test Reports
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit daily/weekly inspection logs documenting concrete curing, reinforcement placement, bulkhead installation, hydrostatic pressure tests, and dewatering operations with photographs and measurements.

requirements.title: Structural Integrity Certification—Caisson Installation
requirements.description: A licensed professional engineer shall certify that the installed caisson meets design specifications, is properly braced, and is ready for load placement and dock construction.

requirements.title: Final Caisson As-Built Documentation
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit as-built drawings, material certifications, test records, and photographic documentation of completed caisson structure for Government record.

SWBS 100 governs the design, fabrication, and installation of the reinforced concrete caisson hull structure that forms the primary containment vessel for the hybrid dry dock. QC oversight ensures structural integrity, material compliance, and adherence to Naval specifications critical to the dock's operational readiness and safety.

requirements.title: Caisson Structural Design & Calculations
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit final structural design calculations, including hydrostatic load analysis, reinforcement layout, and settlement predictions for the caisson hull. Government approval required before fabrication begins.

requirements.title: Reinforcing Steel Shop Drawings
requirements.description: Shop drawings detailing rebar placement, bending schedules, splicing details, and coupler locations for all caisson walls and base slab. Must include tie spacing, cover requirements, and fabrication tolerances.

requirements.title: Concrete Mix Design & Material Certifications
requirements.description: Concrete mix design (including water/cement ratio, admixtures, and air content), plus Material Test Reports for cement, aggregates, and admixtures meeting ASTM and ACI standards.

requirements.title: Concrete Compressive Strength Testing
requirements.description: Cylinder samples cast and tested per ASTM C39 at 7-day and 28-day intervals from each concrete batch placed in caisson structure.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per 100 cubic yards per day, minimum 2 per placement
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Minimum 4,000 psi at 28 days; 7-day strength ≥75% of 28-day design

requirements.title: Reinforcing Steel Installation Inspection
requirements.description: Government QC inspection and sign-off on all reinforcing steel in place, including verification of cover, spacing, lap splices, and coupler installations before concrete placement begins.

requirements.title: Caisson Concrete Placement
requirements.description: Government witness of concrete placement operations, including slump verification, segregation checks, consolidation monitoring, and surface finish acceptance for critical hull surfaces.

requirements.title: Concrete Slump and Air Content Verification
requirements.description: Workability and air entrainment testing performed in accordance with ASTM C143 (slump) and ASTM C173 (air content) on each concrete truck delivery.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per truck load
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Slump 4±1 inch; Air content 6±1.5% (for freeze-thaw exposure)

requirements.title: Caisson Structure Completion Report
requirements.description: Final QC report documenting all test results, non-conformance resolutions, material certifications, and as-built verification that caisson hull meets design specifications and is ready for acceptance.

SWBS 300 governs all electrical systems integral to caisson construction, including power distribution, lighting, grounding, cathodic protection, and cable management for the hybrid dry dock. QC verification ensures compliance with NAVFAC standards, NEC, and naval vessel safety protocols critical to operational readiness.

requirements.title: Electrical Shop Drawings – Caisson Power Distribution
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed shop drawings showing single-line diagrams, feeder routes, cable schedules, switchgear locations, and grounding architecture for all caisson electrical systems including 480V primary distribution.

requirements.title: Product Data – Electrical Cable and Connectors
requirements.description: Manufacturer technical data for all marine-duty cable, penetration connectors, and splices shall be submitted, confirming UV/salt-fog resistance and voltage/current ratings.

requirements.title: Cable Test Certificates – Insulation Resistance
requirements.description: Third-party insulation resistance test reports (megohm meter, 500 VDC minimum) for all caisson feeder cables prior to energization, per IEEE 400.

requirements.title: Grounding Continuity and Resistance Test
requirements.description: Verify all caisson structural steel bonding and ground ring resistance to meet <5 ohm per NAVFAC EH&S standards and IEEE 80. Test frequency once per 500 LF of ground conductor installed.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per 500 LF
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Ground resistance ≤ 5 ohms; continuity ≤ 0.1 ohm between all bonded points

requirements.title: Cathodic Protection System Performance Verification
requirements.description: Measure impressed current output and reference electrode potentials for the caisson hull cathodic protection system to confirm design parameters are met prior to commissioning.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per system
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Hull potential -850 mV to -950 mV (vs. Ag/AgCl); current output within ±10% of design

requirements.title: Electrical Safety and Lockout/Tagout Plan
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit a comprehensive LOTO and electrical safety plan for caisson work, including hazard analysis, confined space protocols, and emergency shutdown procedures.

requirements.title: Electrical Load Analysis and Harmonic Survey Report
requirements.description: Post-installation harmonic and load-flow analysis confirming caisson electrical system operates within THD limits (<5%) and does not degrade shore-power interface.

requirements.title: Electrical System Commissioning Certificate
requirements.description: Licensed marine electrician certification that all caisson electrical systems have been tested, energized, and are fully operational per contract specifications and applicable codes.

SWBS 500 governs all auxiliary systems integrated into the caisson structure, including dewatering systems, drainage galleries, internal piping, and electromechanical support infrastructure. QC oversight ensures proper installation, pressure testing, and operational readiness of these systems critical to dry dock functionality and vessel support.

requirements.title: Caisson Auxiliary Systems Shop Drawings
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit detailed shop drawings for all auxiliary piping, dewatering systems, drainage galleries, valve locations, and support frames integrated into caisson structure. Drawings must show interface points, slopes, and accessibility for maintenance.

requirements.title: Equipment Product Data and Certifications
requirements.description: Submit manufacturer specifications, pressure ratings, material certifications, and performance data for all pumps, valves, strainers, and electromechanical components specified for auxiliary systems.

requirements.title: Piping Hydrostatic Pressure Test
requirements.description: All internal caisson piping systems shall be hydrostatically tested to 1.5× maximum operating pressure and held for 30 minutes with zero leakage.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per system branch; full system test before final closure
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Zero visible leakage; pressure drop ≤2 psi over 30-minute hold

requirements.title: Drainage Gallery and Sump Installation Inspection
requirements.description: Government QC inspection of drainage gallery structure, sump excavation, and filter media installation before sealing. Verify dimensions, grades, and absence of obstructions.

requirements.title: Dewatering System Flow and Capacity Verification
requirements.description: Pump systems shall be tested for rated flow capacity, discharge pressure, and noise levels under no-load and full-load conditions.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per pump installation
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Flow rate ≥95% of rated capacity; discharge pressure within ±5% of design; noise ≤85 dB(A)

requirements.title: Material Test Reports and Mill Certifications
requirements.description: Provide mill test reports for all carbon steel piping, stainless steel components, and elastomer seals used in caisson auxiliary systems per ASTM specifications.

requirements.title: Auxiliary Systems Pre-Closure Punch List and Certification
requirements.description: Contractor submits comprehensive QC report documenting all testing results, corrective actions, and final certification that all auxiliary systems are installed per specification and ready for caisson closure.

sections.sectionTitle: REMOVAL AND SALVAGE OF HISTORIC CONSTRUCTION MATERIALS
sections.aiSummary: Section 02 42 91 governs the careful removal, documentation, and salvage of historic construction materials from Naval Station Neverland's existing dry dock structures prior to TIER 3 hybrid construction. This section ensures compliance with federal historic preservation requirements, environmental regulations, and proper handling of potentially hazardous legacy materials while maintaining chain-of-custody and material traceability for salvaged components.

requirements.title: Historic Materials Survey and Removal Plan
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit a comprehensive survey identifying all historic materials (timber framing, brass fittings, original hardware, structural steel) to be removed, with detailed removal sequencing, protective measures, and storage/disposition plans for each category of material.

requirements.title: Material Documentation and Photographic Record
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit pre-removal photographic documentation, dimensional drawings, and condition assessments of all historic materials, organized by removal phase and tagged for traceability.

requirements.title: Historic Materials Removal Authorization
requirements.description: Government QC shall inspect and approve removal methodology, protective staging, and material segregation setup before any historic material removal commences. No cutting, dismantling, or salvage operations may begin without written authorization.

requirements.title: Asbestos and Lead Paint Sampling (Historic Materials)
requirements.description: Contractor shall conduct laboratory analysis of representative samples from painted wood members, pipe insulation, and caulking prior to removal to identify presence of asbestos or lead-based coatings.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 sample per 500 SF of painted surface; minimum 3 samples per material type
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Asbestos content <1%; lead content classified per EPA guidelines; results guide removal PPE and waste disposal protocol

requirements.title: Removal and Salvage Field Operations
requirements.description: Government QC shall witness removal of all brass fixtures, original hardware, and structural salvage items; verify proper tagging, segregation into salvage containers, and chain-of-custody documentation at each removal phase.

requirements.title: Daily Removal Log and Material Inventory
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit daily logs documenting quantities removed, condition, final disposition (salvage/recycle/disposal), photographs, and any damage or anomalies discovered during removal. Monthly summary report required.

requirements.title: Historic Materials Salvage Certification and Chain-of-Custody
requirements.description: Contractor shall provide signed certification of all salvaged historic materials, including material type, quantity, condition grade, storage location, and transfer/disposition, signed by Contractor and witnessed by Government representative.

requirements.title: Project Completion Record (Historic Salvage)
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit final closeout documentation including as-removed inventory, salvage disposition receipts, recycling/disposal certificates, photographic before-and-after record, and signed compliance statement.

sections.aiSummary: Specification section 03 30 53 — MISCELLANEOUS CAST-IN-PLACE CONCRETE — establishes requirements in accordance with UFGS standards for the Naval Station Neverland dry dock construction project.

Section 31 11 00 governs the removal of vegetation, trees, stumps, roots, and surface obstructions from the dry dock construction footprint at Naval Station Neverland. Proper clearing and grubbing QC ensures site safety, prevents buried debris from compromising foundation integrity, and establishes a clean building plane for subsequent construction phases.

requirements.title: Clearing and Grubbing Plan
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit a detailed clearing and grubbing plan including equipment types, removal methodology, tree/stump locations, disposal routes, and erosion control measures specific to the hybrid dry dock footprint and adjacent naval facilities.

requirements.title: Debris Disposal and Recycling Certification
requirements.description: Contractor shall provide manifest and destination certification for all cleared vegetation, stumps, and excavated debris, including wood chipping/mulching records and/or licensed demolition/disposal facility tipping tickets.

requirements.title: Vegetation and Surface Debris Removal Witness
requirements.description: Government QC shall witness and sign-off on the complete removal of all trees, vegetation, stumps, and surface obstructions from the designated clearing limits prior to grubbing operations.

requirements.title: Subgrade Verification and Compaction Testing
requirements.description: Following grubbing operations, QC shall perform field density tests on exposed subgrade to verify stability and bearing capacity.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per 10,000 SF or minimum 3 locations within dry dock footprint
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Minimum 95% Standard Proctor density; bearing ratio ≥ 5 for wet conditions

requirements.title: Root and Stump Pit Inspection
requirements.description: All tree and stump removal pits shall be inspected for complete root extraction, debris-free status, and proper backfill/compaction before foundation work commences.

requirements.title: Clearing and Grubbing Completion Report
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit a final completion report documenting acreage cleared, volume of material removed, disposal records, compaction test results, and site photographs showing pre- and post-clearing conditions.

requirements.title: Environmental and Erosion Control Documentation
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit erosion control plan, stormwater pollution prevention measures (SWPPP compliance), and environmental protection certifications per Navy/NAVFAC environmental requirements for the Naval Station.

Section 43 11 00.10 governs the design, procurement, installation, and commissioning of off-gas extraction fans, blowers, and vacuum pumps that support the dry dock dewatering and environmental control systems during TIER 3 hybrid construction. Proper QC oversight ensures equipment meets performance ratings, noise limits, vibration standards, and operational reliability under sustained duty cycles inherent to naval vessel maintenance environments.

requirements.title: Off-Gas Fan and Blower Shop Drawings
requirements.description: Contractor shall submit certified shop drawings for all off-gas fans and blowers, including dimensional layouts, mounting details, ductwork connections, vibration isolation specifications, and electrical one-line diagrams. Drawings must clearly identify ASME/ANSI compliance, nameplate data, and interface with dock dewatering systems.

requirements.title: Equipment Product Data and Performance Curves
requirements.description: Manufacturer product data packages shall include performance curves (pressure vs. flow), efficiency ratings, motor specifications, acoustic output (dB at 1 m), vibration limits per ISO 10816, and material certifications for wetted/corrosive environments typical of naval dry docks.

requirements.title: Factory Test and Performance Certificates
requirements.description: Manufacturer shall provide certified test reports documenting performance acceptance testing per AMCA 210 or equivalent, including actual flow rate, static pressure, power input, and noise measurement at rated conditions prior to shipment.

requirements.title: Field Performance and Acceptance Testing
requirements.description: Following installation and before operational turnover, QC shall witness and document field performance testing of all off-gas fans and blowers, including flow verification, pressure drop measurement across ductwork, noise survey at operator stations, and vibration monitoring at bearing supports.
requirements.testFrequency: 1 per major fan/blower unit; spot checks on additional units if fleet exceeds 3 identical units
requirements.acceptanceCriteria: Flow rate ±5% of design; static pressure ±8% of design; sound level ≤85 dBA at 1 m; vibration RMS velocity ≤7.1 mm/s per ISO 10816-3 Zone A

requirements.title: Vibration Isolation Installation Verification
requirements.description: Before any fan unit operates under sustained load, QC must verify that elastomeric mounts and/or spring isolators are installed per manufacturer design, properly aligned, and free of degradation or improper stacking that could compromise vibration dampening.

requirements.title: Ductwork Pressure Decay and Leak Testing
requirements.description: QC shall witness pneumatic testing of all off-gas ductwork systems connected to extraction fans to confirm integrity and assess leakage rates under 6 inches water column design pressure.

requirements.title: Operation, Maintenance, and Troubleshooting Manual
requirements.description: Contractor shall provide compiled O&M documentation including manufacturer instructions for each fan/blower unit, routine maintenance schedules, bearing lubrication intervals, filter change procedures, and a troubleshooting guide for common operational issues specific to naval dry dock duty.

requirements.title: Commissioning and Final Compliance Report
requirements.description: QC Manager shall compile a comprehensive commissioning report documenting all submittals reviewed, tests witnessed, acceptance criteria met, deficiencies corrected, and final sign-off on readiness for operational turnover to Naval Station Neverland.
